place_info.place_editorial_summary: Fun lights on the Morrison Bridge!

metadata.description: Are you one of the thousands of locals and visitors who enjoy the LED lights on the piers of the Morrison Bridge? The Bridge Lighting Program puts the power in your hands. Choose the colors that illuminate the Morrison Bridge 365 nights a year, sparking conversations and raising awareness for causes and events that matter to Portlanders.The Willamette Light Brigade raised the necessary funds, shepherded the design and installation of these low-energy LED fixtures and donated them to the County. The County has contracted with WLB since July 2009 to solicit and process applications from the public and relay approved color choices to the Bridge Shop–on a first come~first served basis. We have been pleased to fulfill requests from individuals and groups with a wide range of causes, events and milestones to celebrate.The Willamette Light Brigade, in partnership with Multnomah County, expanded the Morrison Bridge Lighting Program in 2024 with a seasonal lighting initiative programmed by Chris Herring that celebrates Portland’s changing seasons through vibrant displays on nights not reserved for specific events.You can light the bridge too, check outhttps://lightthebridges.org/to reserve your night now.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Towering 28ft light sculptures features an elegant steel Dandelion by day, and a mesmerizing LED firework by night. An immersive, glowing spectacle.

metadata.description: The Electric Dandelions are a series of stunning light sculptures that bridge the gap between nature and technology. Standing up to 28 feet tall, these installations by Liquid PXL feature a sophisticated design of steel and polycarbonate tubes arranged in a meticulous geodesic sphere.By day, they appear as elegant, towering interpretations of dandelions in full bloom. As the sun sets, they transform into “endless fireworks,” using vibrant LED animations to create a mesmerizing display of light and geometry. Originally debuted at Burning Man in 2016, these sculptures have since illuminated global stages from the River of Light in Liverpool to the Jerusalem Light Festival.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Part immersive sculpture, part sensory instrument, The TUNNEL is more than an installation—it’s a cosmic passage through the imagination.

metadata.description: Some of our most imaginative adventures begin not with a destination, but with a threshold—a door, a portal, a gate, a tunnel. These openings are more than physical structures; they are invitations. They mark the moment before transformation, the pause before the plunge, the quiet breath before the unknown. They ask us to choose curiosity over comfort, to step beyond the ordinary and into the extraordinary. In stories and in life, thresholds symbolize the beginning of a journey—a crossing into a realm where the rules shift, senses heighten, and possibilities expand. Whether literal or metaphorical, these passageways ignite the imagination and challenge us to explore what lies beyond.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Meet "Billy" the loving name given to this giant sparkling illuminated cuttlefish brought to life! Standing 8 feet tall and stretching 15 feet long

metadata.description: Meet a giant sparkling illuminated cuttlefish brought to life! Standing 14 feet tall and stretching 16 feet long (on the pedestal), this captivating creature is crafted from metal, fiberglass, glass, and resin. Its steel skeleton provides a sturdy frame while mosaic glass panels form a skin that dazzles as light filters through, scattering vibrant colors across its surface. Tentacles full of LEDs create a beautiful light show with dazzling colors moving throughout each tentacle.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Glowing flowers bloom where you least expect, transforming ordinary planters into moments of wonder.

metadata.description: Midnight Blooms is an outdoor light installation of glowing flower forms placed throughout the space. Appearing in small, unexpected groupings, the work invites discovery as visitors move through the area.Each cluster feels as if it has quietly emerged after dark, adding atmosphere and warmth to familiar surroundings. By scattering light across the landscape, Midnight Blooms encourages curiosity, reflection, and a slower way of experiencing the night.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Many different sizes of circles working together can create something more. Control a circle and create something bigger.

metadata.description: As you and the other people observing turn the circles on and off, shapes will form and dissipate, creating and transforming the art as everyone works together.

place_info.place_name: Black Light Queen of the Night
place_info.place_editorial_summary: A walk thru black light experience featuring a magical bloom that lasts for just one night

metadata.description: Queen of the Night is a tropical flower that only blooms for one night. They find their stage wandering up vines on palm trees. Small cotton buds forecast their locations and as they mature a giant 12″ flower explodes like fireworks in the night sky. On nights where there are many blooms a sweet scent fills the air. Here I hope to share some of the magic of them with you.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Quantum Jungle playfully visualizes Quantum Physics phenomena on a wall filled with thousands of touch-sensitive springs and lights.

artists: Quantum Jungle at World Trade Center
metadata.description: When touching any of its springs, Quantum Jungle creates a visualisation of a quantum particle and simulates how it behaves using Schrödinger’s Equation, demonstrating concepts such as superposition, interference, wave-particle duality, and quantum waveform collapse.Metal springs that wobble, dance, and push back against touch, measured with precise sensors and visualized in bright and fluid colors on large LED arrangements that defy common grid structures.The scientific aspect is woven into all of these interactive features. Through a collaboration with quantum scientists from Universities in Finland and Italy, we’ve created an engaging art piece that visualises quantum physics in a fun and approachable way.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Join the spirit of the Shadow Forest. Who knows what you will find.

metadata.description: The installation redefines the nature of the Pacific Northwest as perceived through the shadows of the forest around us. The shapes and elements we see can be defined by the absense of light. Layering color, diffusion, refraction and reflection all play into the perception of the object.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Brains are overrated! Come melt your mind as you peer into the Mind Melter 6000! Note: Please clean up your own melted mind. Mop and bucket provided.

metadata.description: The Mind Melter 6000 is a kaleidoscope on a mission to gently scramble your brain in the best possible way. Illuminated on both the inside and outside with shifting, shimmering colors, it transforms every glance into a cosmic light show. Two sturdy handles invite visitors to take control—turn them, and the entire universe inside seems to twist, fold, and bloom into new patterns. As if that weren’t enough visual mischief, the piece also hides four infinity mirrors, each one stretching light into impossible depths. Together, they create a hypnotic experience that feels part science, part magic, and entirely unforgettable.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: GLOW WITH THE FLOW invites you to find beauty amid chaos. This interactive fluid simulation turns your movement into a masterpiece, so start swirling!

metadata.description: This debut installation by Portland artist Radian is designed to get you out of your head and into your body, out of a moment and into a movement. GLOW WITH THE FLOW encourages people of all ages to let go of what they needn’t carry (all the little things) and simply appreciate the magic they create.This dynamic exhibit can be enjoyed solo or with multiple people at once. Dancing, spinning, jumping, and laughing are encouraged – just remember to be mindful of others around you!

place_info.place_editorial_summary: This piece explores the feel of resilience; to burn, not be destroyed, and to return; Facets and Small Matters is about what survives to be reborn.

metadata.description: Facets and Small Matters is a story of resilience—what survives, what transforms, what holds light even after burning. The foundation is charred wood: shou sugi ban, fire-treated until its surface cracks into a cellular pattern. The texture isn’t damage, it’s evidence of survival! The fingerprint of fire itself—a Voronoi pattern written in carbon, contains an advanced volumetric display. This Voronoi pattern repeats in the patterned cuts in the faces of the two flanking columns, each of which holds a highly-technical display element. The lessons: Sometimes, beauty and expression come from pain. Sometimes, what seems ruined can be redeemed. And just maybe, when we think something is over…maybe that’s not really the end, after all…

place_info.place_editorial_summary: This artwork uses light to explore how small changes grow into something larger. Move your hands over the control area to change scale.

metadata.description: Light Begins Small is an interactive light installation exploring scale, attention, and gradual change. A single sculptural tower, about 12 feet tall, rises from twisting steel rods and cross-braced metal. Near the top, a translucent panel made from recycled milk and water jugs catches projected light, carrying faint traces of their former use.Projected visuals draw from water, air, and flowing currents, unfolding slowly in patterns that never fully repeat. At a nearby control area, visitors use small hand movements to change the scale of the imagery—moving closer to reveal more detail or pulling back to see broader forms. The interaction is simple and open-ended, rewarding curiosity and patience.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A playful light and sound installation where glowing droplets echo mitosis—responding to button touch and merging into a shared musical conversation.

metadata.description: Dew Drops is an interactive installation of eight glowing, inflatable sculptures floating overhead like oversized drops of water caught mid-split. Inspired by both dew droplets and mitosis—the moment when a single cell divides and becomes something new—each form feels alive and responsive.Below, a central control stand invites visitors to press illuminated buttons linked to each sculpture. Every press triggers a musical tone while its matching dew drop shifts color and movement. As more people join in, the sounds and lights overlap into a shared composition, echoing how cells communicate and work together. Through playful interaction, light, and sound, Dew Drops celebrates connection, collaboration, and collective creation.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Ascension is an 8-foot mirrored pyramid experienced with an AR headset, revealing intricate geometric patterns and infinite vertical light.

artists: Ascension AR at World Trade Center
metadata.description: Ascension is an 8-foot-tall mirrored pyramid installation that invites viewers into a contemplative journey of light, geometry, and transformation. Constructed from precision mirrors and internal illumination, the sculpture creates the illusion of infinite vertical depth. An interactive augmented-reality layer expands the experience, allowing participants to engage with intricate geometric patterns, luminous pathways, and evolving visual structures that respond to movement. Through AR interaction, Ascension becomes a living monument—bridging physical form and digital space—guiding audiences through a meditative exploration of elevation, perception, and higher-dimensional awareness.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Walk through a tiny house transformed into a disco cloud forest where enchanted birds, flowers, and mysterious beings keep watch over nightly revels

artists: art by Anne Jennings Paris at the All Electric Tiny House
metadata.description: Visit this family-friendly realm, where a magical forest is brought to life by disco-ball clouds, LED waterfalls, and a black-light apothec-aerie cave where birds watch over their enchanted eggs and where moonlight streams in from the party above. The art installation also hints at the mystery behind the Lightkeeper, a host who keeps the forest safe and who shares the light with his guests, some human, some imaginary. Made from found materials, encaustic wax, clay, fabric, foam, wire, wood, and paper, this installation is a place where stories are born.Visit the installation inside the PGE All Electric Home parked on Salmon Street next to the World Trade Center hub during the first weekend of the festival only.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Radiolarian is a mobile, interactive, scale model of a microscopic plankton called a radiolarian.

metadata.description: Radiolarian is an inflatable scale model of a microscopic ocean dwelling organism of the same name. Radiolarians are single celled organisms that create a silica exoskeleton that is radially symmetric. They drift with the ocean currents and are a unique species that use photosynthesis as well as eating other smaller organisms. Radiolarian, the art project, is an inflatable and mobile replica of this microscopic creature, sitting on an electric wheelchair base and lit with hundreds of LEDs.*Radiolarian will be roaming around the Festival. Catch it at Big Pink duringBling Your Thingon Saturdays from 6 – 8 PM*

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A friendly inflatable garden serpent who glows from within. Stroll under his arches and discover the images hidden in his scales.

metadata.description: Sir Penti is part of Soft World, a friendly realm where small things are big and big things are small. Sir Penti is the creation of Genevieve Hildebrand-Chupp, an experienced inflatable artist whose work has appeared in previous festivals, as well as FathomPDX, Arts Aglow, and more.Sir Penti debuted in 2025 at Cully Fest, and the 2026 Portland Winter Light Festival marks his first outing as a fully lit creature, showing off the hand silk-screened images on his stripes, also made by the artist.You can walk under his arches, tap at his scales, and give him gentle hugs.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: In a dark world, it only takes a little bit of light to make a big difference. Take turns adding your own light to this interactive display.

metadata.description: What adds tiny shimmers of light to your world? In this interactive piece, viewers flip switches to add and remove light from the full design, shifting solitary outlines of isolation and darkness into colorful images of connection, collaboration, and joy. Bringing in elements of shadow puppetry and theatrical lighting design, “Tiny Shimmers” gives viewers the opportunity to create a dynamic collage of feelings and experiences using light, color, and shape. The darkness is transformed from a stagnant trap into a playful canvas, helping us to imagine new ways of bringing tiny shimmers of light into the world.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: 'within a grain' explores how tiny elements shape vast systems, weaving light, memory, energy, and our role as both consumer and steward.

metadata.description: Salt is tiny made of minuscule crystals and grains yet it shapes worlds. A single grain preserves food and ancestral memory, erodes stone, powers ecosystems, and now stores the sun’s energy in molten batteries. This installation transforms the grain into an illuminated, refracting and reflected light, dynamic through cycles of charge and release. The work echoes blue-carbon marshes along Oregon’s coast, where plants and sediments store centuries of atmospheric carbon like living batteries. Mirrored panes fold the viewer into this continuum, revealing our reflection as both consumer and steward. Through light, ‘Within a Grain’ invites us inside the smallest crystal to witness how the tiniest presence can create vast impact.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Saily rotating selection of music-related anagrams originating from the name of a music club "Paradise Blue" in the windows of World Trade Center.
A celebration of music and color.
A larger than life lampshade inviting reflection, wonder, and quiet togetherness.

metadata.description: The intention behind this piece is to explore the ways in which light and shadow interact. Not only in the physical world, but also to reflect on their significance in the inner world of our hearts and minds.We hope this piece serves as a reminder that light requires shadow to create beauty—each one is meaningless without the contrast of the other. It is an expression of the duality of existence.Sarah Meyer and her partner, Brandon Shaw are Portland based artists who enjoy creating liminal spaces with reused and recycled materials. They are members of the Portal Arts Collective and can often be found working on art installations at local and regional events.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A glass threshold can create moments of clarity and opposing moments of unease. Take a moment, sit or stand and experience being in the threshold.

metadata.description: This threshold is a symbol of intentionally honoring change to come. How our past and future shapes us. This particular piece symbolizes the simplistic shape of glass circles. Signifying many thresholds or moments of change. They will all come together to create one big threshold.Walking through this doorway or sitting with it, can allow enlightening moments for each person who participates. I invite people to experience the in-between. Close their eyes in this threshold and hone in on their senses. Feel the constriction or decompression of change.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: The Chromosphere is interactive, animated, LED-covered sphere that you can stand inside and affect the patterns with sound.

metadata.description: The Chromosphere is interactive, 5-ft diameter, animated, LED-covered sphere that participants are encouraged to stand inside. The installation is sound-interactive, and contains microphone that is used to affect animations on the sphere. People outside the sphere can watch the person inside as they sing and have fun playing with the lights using sound!

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Whisper your wish into a microphone and watch it move along a thread of light to illuminate the Wish Amplifier in a dazzling display.

metadata.description: Deep in Forest Park, our team unearthed a mysterious relic. Research revealed it was crafted by a local collective of witches, adepts, and mystics following their successful levitation of the Pentagon in 1967. Restoring it in our secret lab, we discovered the device’s purpose: to amplify good energy in a big way by harvesting something that seems small — the whisper of a wish. The device harnesses the unseen forces behind every intention, growing brighter with each heart’s desire.For reasons unknown, the collective hid the Wish Amplifier in the forest for future generations to find. It serves as a beacon, lighting the way toward a more hopeful and inspired future. Perhaps they knew we would need it now more than ever.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Moving bodies are transformed into vibrant, luminous films that dance across buildings, enchanting audiences of all ages.

metadata.description: Live-action footage of movers and dancers was captured and then transformed using the artist’s custom software. Snowflakes and flowers emerge and flow from their movements, creating a visceral sense of what feels like to be inside the dance. Large-scale projections of these films amplify their sense of magic and wonder.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: 1-Sir Penti: Friendly, inflatable serpent 2-Infinite Worlds: Glowing tower of stories 3-Yum Yum's Feastival: Whimsical picnic for lizards and ants

metadata.description: In Soft World, big things are little, and little things are big. Visit inside the World Trade Center during the festival.“Sir Penti,” a friendly, inflatable grass serpent looms over the World Trade Center’s interior space, his green and yellow stripes illuminated from within. His scales glow with silk screened images from nature that invite children to come closer and identify leaves, butterflies, and more. “Infinite Worlds Diorama Tower” features visual stories from 7 Portland-area artists. This 5’6″ tower gives visitors a glimpse into 12 mysterious worlds, each with a unique lighting scheme.“Yum Yum’s Feastival” is a soft space for playing with a lively lizard and friendly ants among larger-than-life picnic leftovers.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Partybot loves you so much they need to express it with huge animated billboards! Bask in the warm glow of Partybot’s loving affirmations!

metadata.description: Partybot loves all biological beings and seeks to uplift their spirits with joyful revelry. For years, the Bot has been popping up at festivals and celebrations, encouraging humans to dance, party, and savor their carbon-based existence.*Hint – there might be another Partybot spotting in PDX – see if you can find it.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Lie under the Portal and let it transport you to another space and time.

metadata.description: Portal is a steel sculpture focused on creating a large 3D light environment while minimizing the visual space taken up by structure. It is a sculpture that is intended to feel otherworldly by day and provide an immersive visual experience by night.Lying down next to a friend or stranger to view the Portal creates a shared intimate experience, sparking connection.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: SUBSURFACE LIGHT is a 3D projection-mapping animation that transforms a wall to reveal what lies just beneath the surface and what emerges at viewers.

metadata.description: SUBSURFACE LIGHT, a 3D projection-mapping animation that transforms a wall into other things, other spaces. Inspired by the festival’s theme, All the Little Things, the piece focuses on what lies just beneath the surface—hidden systems, subtle rhythms, and tiny actors that make up the unseen foundations of life and cities alike.By using the wall’s texture and layout as a canvas, the projection reveals a layered ecosystem of movement: working bees, pulsing mycelium, whispering root networks, light bugs, and neuron-like filaments. These elements come together in an evolving loop that breathes and flows—honoring the tiny, tireless efforts that hold everything together.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Onward and Outward affirms a universe where growth, change, and expansion are constant, and where every ending feeds into another beginning.

metadata.description: Walt Whitman’s quote “All goes onward and outward, nothing collapses…” was the inspiration for this art installation. With nearly 2,000 LEDs, the sculpture undulates in a cyclical motion that mirrors the author’s belief that every apparent ending is merely a shift into a new state. Nothing truly disappears; it becomes part of a larger, ongoing process.Visitors are encouraged to consider their own place within this unending flow. Just as the LEDs never truly extinguish but shift into new formations, so do the moments, relationships, and experiences in our lives. The sculpture, in its ceaseless pulse, offers a visual reminder of this truth: that every transformation is part of a larger, evolving whole.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Pixel Drift explores the space between pixels and their connection to the analog world we live in, we invite you to come be part of that journey.

metadata.description: The core purpose of “Pixel Drift” is to probe the creative potential of limitations. What happens when a painter only has two colors to use? Or if ants only built horizontal tunnels?You can interact with this exhibit by scanning a large QR code in the window, thereby accessing two distinct digital experiences. The first uses generative AI to build environments. The second experience encourages you to place physical objects into the digital space, symbolizing how small elements impact a larger whole.The entire display is bound by a strict limit of 12,887,068 pixels, which are constantly randomized and recycled to manifest new objects. This digital ecosystem visualizes the beauty of constraints, ultimately exploring the endless opportunities that are all around us.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Twisty Maurice returns to PDXWLF with a glowing lattice of light, mirrors, and motion where math and code ripple into hypnotic patterns.

metadata.description: Light blooms, spirals, and shifts in mesmerizing patterns, drawing you into a calm, meditative experience. As you move, the sculpture seems to move with you; curious, playful, and oddly comforting. Oscillara invites joy, surprise, and a quiet sense of connection.Oscillara started with a gift of over 100 PVC pipes, a spark that led the artists to explore modular geometry and light. Each pipe became a luminous cell, arranged into triangular clusters, hexagons, and adaptable units that can grow or shift to suit a space.

place_info.place_name: Workshop: PGE Project Zero Makerspace at World Trade Center
place_info.place_editorial_summary: Step into the PGE Makerspace forest and etch your own glowing animal art to take home! Suggested for attendees ages 8+.

metadata.description: Inspired by the magic of forests and light, the PGE Project Zero Makerspace invites visitors to create their own piece of wearable art. Guests will etch a forest animal of their choice onto a light-up pin that they can wear, blending creativity, design, and simple electrical concepts in a fun, take-home activity.This experience is designed to spark curiosity and imagination while introducing youth and families to hands-on making in a welcoming, nighttime festival setting.**Recommended for ages 8 and up, with younger participants welcome alongside an adult. This activity will use lithium coin batteries which present a swallowing risk.**

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Synthesthesia Crystals is an light art sculpture of massive, music reactive infinity crystals!

metadata.description: The Synthesthesia Crystals are Inspired by the natural crystal forms in nature, and the mirror geometry produces a mesmerizing pattern of light and dark. Each cluster has an instrument which controls the color of the patterns within. This encourages the viewer to take part in the creation of the art. The color of the lights within the Crystals is connected to the harmony of what is being played on the instrument, using our special Synthesthesia music visualizer. This interactivity allows you to connect with light in a tactile, physical way, allowing illuminating the environment as well as experiencing the deep connection between music and light.ON VIEW & FOR SALE You can find select works by this artist for sale on consignment at official Portland Winter Light Festival merch booths at select anchor locations during the Festival.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Stepping through the Portal invites a shift of perspective. Wonder is not distant, but always here. Waiting only to be seen with fully present eyes!

metadata.description: Welcome to the Portal of Perception. We didn’t create it as an object to admire, but as a space where play, presence, and connection can quietly rise. You’re invited to notice how even a small shift in viewpoint can open something new. Breathe. Feel yourself arrive in this moment; aware of the space and the people beside you. Then as you walk through the Portal notice how the light, the shadows, and your own awareness rearrange the world. What you discover isn’t in the steel or the glow, but in the act of perceiving itself. Wonder lives here — not in things, but in the way we awaken to them.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Electric Avenue is a series of LED-neon-tube signs in the look and feel of cyberpunk, retro-futuristic neon-light-covered streets.

metadata.description: Electric Avenue is a number of LED-neon-tube signs to help recreate the look and feel of cyberpunk, retro-futuristic neon-light-covered streets of places like Hong Kong, Neo Tokyo, or other such places.The signs feature frame animations of real neon signs, but utilize LEDs inside, enabling all kinds of colors and color animations not possible with real neon. This is the retro-future!

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Immerse yourself in RAIN by artist Dave Meeker. Share in the joy of rain, the abundance of which is one of the things that makes Portland unique.

metadata.description: The installation of RAIN considers rain in its many guises: gentle, steady, torrential, pouring, gully washer, mist…. RAIN is a defining feature of Oregon. It forms the substance of our rivers, lakes and streams and nourishes our verdant forests, which provide habitat for an incredible diversity of flora and fauna.No one has considered RAIN better than Langston Hughes in his poem April Rain Song.Let the rain kiss you Let the rain beat upon your head with silver liquid drops Let the rain sing you a lullaby The rain makes still pools on the sidewalk The rain makes running pools in the gutter The rain plays a little sleep song on our roof at night And I love the rain.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Delicate & nearly gone. Explore an AR scavenger hunt that brings threatened native plants & their place in our ecosystems back into view.

metadata.description: Ghost Flora is an interactive AR scavenger hunt that brings to life native plants of the Pacific Northwest—some long extinct, others on the brink of disappearing. Hidden throughout the Winter Lights Festival are illustrated markers that act as portals to lost and threatened ecosystems. When scanned with a phone, each ghost plant is brought to life in AR. Participants will learn about these delicate life forms and the vital roles they play—and in some cases, still play—in our local ecosystems. Collect them all to unlock a chance to win special prizes.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A glowing chest that moves and opens/closes its lid. Inside are large teeth and a floppy tongue. Beneath the chest are many small feet.

metadata.description: The Luggage is a traveling wooden chest that scuttles about on tiny feet and frightens people that get in its way. Behind the wooden slats that make up the sides and top of the chest are many LEDs, causing the Luggage to emit colorful light. When it opens its lid, large teeth are revealed and a tongue flops out. Inside the chest are a few very bright LEDs that bathe the area in front of it. The Luggage is accompanied by wizards of questionable competence, who may be there just to chat-up strangers.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Engage with a journey across imagined worlds as seen through costumes created by Portland Opera. Each costume tells a story!

place_info.place_name: Live Music: Portland Opera Presents: Queen of the Night Aria at World Trade Center
place_info.place_editorial_summary: A timeless classic on the World Trade Center Plaza: Mozart's Queen of the Night Aria, presented in sparkling lights by Portland Opera!

metadata.description: Enjoy a virtuoso performance by soprano Madeleine Ross, lighting up the night in a dress and headdress by Portland artist Ivy Thompson. The Queen of the Night Aria is arguably the most famous melody from Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart’s The Magic Flute, and 2026 marks the debut of Portland Opera at PDX Winter Lights Festival.

place_info.place_name: DJ: Simy Garay – Music Experience at World Trade Center
place_info.place_editorial_summary: Music from all around the world.

metadata.description: I will be spinning music from all around the world with some amazing light installations.

place_info.place_name: Juggling and More: Spencer Sprocket Extravaganza at World Trade Center
place_info.place_editorial_summary: Spencer Sprocket's back, brighter and bolder than ever! Juggling, magic, comedy, more lights, and all-ages fun for the whole family!

metadata.description: Spencer Sprocket has been practicing having fun for over 10 years. This full-time entertainer can be found up and down the West Coast performing at birthday parties, stage shows, festivals, and anywhere else he can bring a smile to people’s faces. Using multi-prop juggling, sleight-of-hand magic, and comedy, he engages with audiences and provides everyone with a unique and memorable experience.

place_info.place_name: Roaming costumed creatures: Butteyes at World Trade Center
place_info.place_editorial_summary: The Butteyes are alien-like creatures that dance, prance, and delight audiences of all ages with their large glowing green eyes and playful curiosity.

metadata.description: The Butteyes emerged at an Echo Theater Company rehearsal in 2013. Students were spinning on trapeze wearing flowing skirts when a 10-year old girl got into a downward dog shape with the skirt over her body and just her rear end poking through the top. As she scuttled around the theater, we realized how funny this costume could be with googly eyes affixed to the buns of the operator. In this moment of playful, silly exploration, Echo’s mascot the Butteye was born.In 2022, teens from Echo’s Zig Zag ensemble added LEDs and introduced glowing Butteyes to PDXWLF. The Butteyes represent how innovation, looking at things differently, and providing space for silliness and exploration can all come together to make the world more magical and quirky.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Vision of the future world where humans and human shaped robots coexist together in peace and harmony.

metadata.description: We are experiencing a turning point in mankind history. Thanks to AI and modern robotics development we are on the edge of time to live along with highly developed, intelligent machines – androids that can look like humans and be far more efficient in every aspect. My animation is telling the story about creating androids, living in harmony with them and fulfilling the dreams. It’s about tolerance, acceptance and imagination. Who knows, maybe creating artificial but more developed humans is a time of rebirth for mankind and time to answer again what is it to be human in its deepest core.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: The Portal invites curiosity into action—an interactive gateway that asks: Do you step into the unknown, or turn back? Can you unlock its mysteries?

artists: The Portal at Pioneer Courthouse Square
metadata.description: THE PORTAL suggests a transformative journey, but it begins with a simple spark of curiosity: What is that—and where does it go? From ancient myth to modern pop culture, portals mark the threshold between the known and the unknown, inviting us to imagine what lies beyond. The installation explores curiosity at a grand scale—the same instinct behind humanity’s greatest discoveries and darkest mistakes. When faced with the unknown, do you step forward? A 16-foot immersive structure is activated through a custom interactive console that reveals hidden features controlling a 16-foot circular video screen with dynamic LED lighting and synchronized audio.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A simple geode can reveal the many wonders of light. You are the explorer of light, learning what it has traveled so far to share with you.

metadata.description: Light has traveled billions of miles to meet us at the end of its journey. Not only does it allow us to see the world, but it brings so much more to our lives. Light and shadow reveal to us the beauty of shape and form. The colors of illumination evoke powerful emotions, unlocking core memories. We can feel the warmth and power of light wrapping around us, encouraging us to continue. Light may be small and without substance, but it profoundly affects every piece of matter on the little rock we call home. A geode is like a curio cabinet for light. The edge of a crystal lets loose a glint of light as you shift your perspective. The facets offer deep, wonderful colors while reflecting a picture of your own inquisitive self.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Your own cellular geometry's structure. Step inside and be reminded that every cell in your body is setup to receive and transmit love.

metadata.description: The fundamental geometry of space, crystals and the whole universe exists also within every cell of our bodies. First inspired by contemplating the crystal lattice structure of gallium nitride LEDs emitting blue photons and the interference patterns they create, the work evolved as we recognized this geometry closely resembles Buckminster Fuller’s Vector Equilibrium from his sphere packing experiments. By replacing the spheres with sine functions, this sculpture bridges quantum-scale phenomena with human-scale experience—a reminder that every cell in our bodies is designed to receive and transmit love, connecting us to universal geometric principles spanning from quantum photon emission to biological structures of life itself. Step Inside.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: How does one keep thousands of festival goers warm and happy?

metadata.description: Ivan’s Fire Balls have kept the frost off attendees of the Portland Winter Light Festival for years now and are a gathering point for folks to warm up, come together and listen to each other. These were originally built for “What The Festival,” as gathering and warming centers.Ivan makes mostly nonfunctional things these days. Ivan creates furniture and sculptures with all kinds of materials and intentions for public and private spaces around the country.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Mechateuthis is a crowd controllable kinetic sculpture of a mechanical giant squid.

metadata.description: Turning the cranks on the perimeter fence controls the squid’s eyes, arms, tentacles, beak, fins, light colors, and more! Each crank does something different. For the full effect, get someone on every crank at once!

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A twisting predatory plant that shoots fire in 3 directions, come stick your hands in their mouth to find the interactive trigger for the flame effect

metadata.description: From the depths of the earth grows the Trippity Twitchit. Enchanting the curious with its colours and reflections, beware it’s 9 heads, for they will cause distraction and wonder. If treated correctly and with bravery, you may be in for a fiery surprise.A veritable monsters’ garden of metalwork. These larger-than-life, “Marioesque” venus fly traps are a combination of plant, animal, steel, fire, and interactivity, and will certainly draw some attention. Each evening the plant comes to life, shimmering light from within and all around, inviting everyone to activate its 3 separate flame effects.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Tell the artist one thing that you love and they will scribe it onto the wall of ice. Over time, this wall will be filled will things people love.

metadata.description: Despite how dark things may appear in your life, there is always something to be grateful for. This wall of solid, clear ice invites you to share that one thing you love and to be scribed on. This will be added with the other things that people love. Because when small things are grouped together, you will find out that the world full of abundance. Sometimes a little nudge is all it takes to help you know where to look for all the love that surrounds us.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Tangle yourself underneath a vibrant canopy of LED rainbow crotchet webs. CAUTION: You may make new friends in your attempt to escape the Love Diodes.

artists: Love Diodes at Pioneer Courthouse Squar
metadata.description: Join the small creatures caught in the crochet web of love as you find yourself immersed in a technicolor oasis. 360º LED lights illuminate handmade rainbow crochet umbrellas, creating a dancing vibrance that attracts all towards its thrall. Get lost in the webs, join in conversation, and feel the light emit within yourself.Originally envisioned by Curtis McHenry, this artwork is dedicated to creating a safe space to bring people together during the hustle and bustle of a busy festival. This oasis invites you to step inside with an open heart and a willingness to listen.ON VIEW & FOR SALE You can find select works by this artist for sale on consignment at official Portland Winter Light Festival merch booths at select anchor locations during the Festival.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Bloom is a spinning cylinder of LEDs that spreads out under centrifugal force to make the shape of a lily.

metadata.description: Bloom is a cylinder with over 4000 full-color LEDs. When the cylinder spins, its top edges splay out under centrifugal force to create a curved funnel shape resembling a lily bloom. The LEDs display vivid, animated patterns as the drive motor slows down and speeds up to close and re-open the bloom.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: The Ministry of Stupid Ideas, aka MSI, presents the interactive Eternal Flame of Stupid Ideas.

metadata.description: The Eternal Flame of Stupid Ideas aka MSI-EFSI returns to the PDXWLF allowing participants to submit their thoughts and stupid ideas which if deemed exceptional or befittingly stupid the obelisk will react with a poof of flame signifying its approval.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Peer into the flickering scenes ignited by your presence, as you follow this spiraling journey through time, maybe spying a PNW icon along the way.

metadata.description: You are not lost, but captivated by the mysteries of time that draw you in. Do not be wary of the trail ahead with its gentle curves that breathe you in. Wander through time, but not too fast, or you might miss the familiar PNW guide lurking around.Glimpse into this array of portals, capturing little moments in time. Winding back to the beginning of time, you will find you are not alone; you are joined by a big-footed companion who has been spotted meandering through the ages. Watch as the stills of eras past come to life, illuminated by your visit as you follow in your guide’s large footsteps.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Wish is an illuminated kinetic sculpture of a dandelion in various states of development!

metadata.description: Childhood experiences with their golden hues, sawtooth leaves and sudden transformations inspired the light and movement displayed by this beautiful and shy flower. Instantly recognizable glowing sawtooth leaves extend out around the base of the giant flower. This year, the flower is in bloom, with yellow light up petals which are articulated to open and close. If, as is our wish, this piece returns in the future, the flower will have gone to seed, and will feature removable light-up seed florets.ON VIEW & FOR SALE You can find select works by this artist for sale on consignment at official Portland Winter Light Festival merch booths at select anchor locations during the Festival.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Frogzilla is a tribute to tactical frivolity and the protesting spirit of Portland. Frogzilla brings the warmth and light in dark times.

metadata.description: Frogzilla is the unlikely hero we need now more than ever! Hippity hopping through the streets and laughing in the face of fascism.They bring the light and levity in darkness and remind us that: FROGS TOGETHER STRONG

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A moonlit reservoir

metadata.description: Terrene Enfoldment is a meditative portal of reflections and reveries. Embrace uncertainty and traverse through.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: LED illumination has been added around the top of some of Portland Streetcar vehicles, providing a rolling installation of winter light.

metadata.description: The PortlandStreetcarwill be free beginning at 5 PM on February 6, 7, 13 and 14.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A pulsing piece that brings you into the organic landscape of care and what it means to be part of a community
LED Happiness on your table or desk.

metadata.description: Enjoy the display of the LUMIO LED Tabletop Plant.A premium decorative RGB lighting foliage designed for upscale interior decoration, retail displays, hospitality environments, and commercial installations. Adjustable brightness and speed with 120 pre-programmed patterns to enhance your environment and mood.

place_info.place_name: DJ: djMOM: Total Sum Of The Works at Pioneer Courthouse Square
place_info.place_editorial_summary: The soundtrack, score, and soundscape of Pioneer Courthouse Square.

metadata.description: This is a multifaceted musical role. Sometimes I’m djing tunes to enhance the experiences throughout the Square. Sometimes I’m playing the songs for the various talented flow arts performances. Other times I’m the sound engineer for any live musical acts that may grace the Square.My project is the honor of supplementing the lights with sounds. I am a cameo to the main actors, which are the many art pieces on display, and the supporting cast, who are the many humans who’ve worked very hard to create amazingly beautiful experiences for citizens of our dear City of Portland.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: TiMM is a digital art collective made up of audio visual professionals and tech geeks exploring generative, interactive and immersive art.

metadata.description: TiMM is the brain child of creative technologist Luke Norby. He quickly found a kindred spirit in Green Century Electronic Recycling owner Chris Regis. Together they developed a plan to form a creative technology non-profit, an art organization exhibiting new and emerging digital artists coupled with a community outreach arm providing a platform for the next generation of creative technologists and A/V professionals.Energized by the new venture, Luke recruited two colleagues, Richard Laws and John Coletta. Together they created T.i.M.M. 1.0, a small but multi-faceted exhibit for the 2025 Portland Winter Light Festival. In the year since TiMM has appeared at the Pickathon Music Fest, hosted its first exhibition and tripled in size.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A living projection generating visuals in real time, shaped by human movement and those interacting with it.

metadata.description: Living Light is a real-time generative projection built using TouchDesigner and StreamDiffusion. The visuals are generated from a set of pre-selected text prompts inspired by “All the Little Things”. These prompts describe small visual elements and qualities that the system continuously combines and reinterprets.A Kinect depth camera reads people in the space as moving silhouettes. As visitors move in front of the projection, their motion and form influence how the imagery changes on the wall. Color, texture, and form shift continuously based on what is happening in the space.Living Light looks at how simple motion and visual forms can interact to create something new over time.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: VHS loop time piece
Light emerges from a branching form. Neurolith unites technology and craft, shaped by decades of experience into a totemic presence.

metadata.description: Neuroluth is a luminous form shaped by countless individual elements working together. All these little branching structures echo natural systems—roots, neurons, and vessels—where strength and intelligence emerge from accumulation rather than force. Created through a union of advanced LED technology, careful handcraft, and er…a lot of plywood, this piece reflects decades of shared experience. Familiar yet unfamiliar, it feels both ancient and contemporary, inviting viewers to consider how complexity, patience, and collaboration give rise to light.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: LED Tree Sculpture

metadata.description: Its color shift from night to night, reflecting the exchange, conversation, and shared creative energy.A place to meet, linger, and glow together.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: These LED floor panel surfaced benches and tables are designed to be unfamiliar in a familiar form, using some of the newest tech in a simple way.

metadata.description: The initial design was born out of necessity. The broken asphalt courtyard at the entrance to Pickathon 2025 was not conducive to the level surface requirements of these Planar donated LED floor panels. Volunteer Pickathon carpenters built benches to house them. They were an immediate hit. At the Tiny Interactive Media Museum we’ve expanded on the design to now include several smaller individual seats or tables, and a larger table meant for communal gathering.The furniture catches your attention quickly. It’s simple construction from untreated lumber sits in juxtaposition to the technology it supports. The video collages moving across the pieces carry several messages, not initially apparent. We invite you to sit awhile and rest.

place_info.place_name: DJ: Space Bass at Glow Bar
place_info.place_editorial_summary: Come with us on a journey that will transcend time and space using the power of sound and motion.

metadata.description: Music, dance and art are extremely powerful tools for building community. Space Bass uses these tools to create safe and inclusive spaces where everyone is welcome, no matter what galaxy you’re from.“Where words fail, music speaks.”

place_info.place_editorial_summary: These Archways are symbolic of the passing of time. With Illustrative silhouettes carved into each arch, you can discover a progressive narrative.

metadata.description: Living in the Anthropocene, we are acutely aware of the profound and often irreversible impact our actions have on the planet. The Industrial Revolution ushered in an era of unprecedented technological advancement, fueled by a belief in limitless progress and the power to shape the world. However, this era has brought us to a critical juncture. We must now confront the consequences of our past actions and re-evaluate. Now we must ask how to live in accord with nature, not as regression but as a new kind of innovation worthy of our future. In this sculpture, each archway alludes to either the, past present, or future, together they create a hopeful narrative for what life can become with some consideration.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Get lost in a hidden parallel dimension inhabited by a forest of glowing, noodle-like creatures.

artists: Parallel Dimension No. 58400 at Big Pink
metadata.description: Step into an alien realm populated by a grove of luminous noodle-like creatures, where lights and sound are the neurotransmitters between organisms. Time and space are intermixed as the motion of lights turns into shapes in this giant volumetric display. But watch out… take a wrong turn and you may find yourself in another dimension.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Luxtriplicata is an interactive light installation where your touch transforms white light through prisms into shifting colors and playful motion.

metadata.description: Luxtriplicata is a hands-on, family-friendly light installation that merges art, science, and play. At its center, a rotatable plate embedded with cube-shaped prisms catches a beam of white light, breaking it into shifting patterns. As visitors explore, their touch turns these beams into vivid projections that move across the space, forming a mesmerizing 360° universe of color and motion.This is not a work to be merely observed; it is one to be explored, reshaped, and reimagined. In Luxtriplicata, the audience becomes both artist and participant, discovering the beauty of light through playful experimentation and shared wonder.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Partybot loves you so much they need to express it with huge animated billboards! Bask in the warm glow of Partybot’s loving affirmations!

metadata.description: Partybot loves all biological beings and seeks to uplift their spirits with joyful revelry. For years, the Bot has been popping up at festivals and celebrations, encouraging humans to dance, party, and savor their carbon-based existence.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Heron's Embrace curates an alluring space that makes one feel small, and demands those under its wings consider their place in the natural world.

metadata.description: “Heron’s Embrace” depicts a huge blue heron engaging in hunting behavior. By creating shade with its outstretched wings while standing perfectly still, the bird is better able to spot prey, lulling frogs and fish into a false sense of security. Standing over twenty feet tall with an equally impressive wingspan, our heron eyes viewers with hypnotic intensity as they are welcomed to sit on lily pad cushions. Constructed from vintage Volkswagen parts and over 200 feet of post-consumer plastic, as well as shade cloth gleaned from past Burning Man projects, The Heron welcomes you into the introspective realm of the shadows.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Meet your digital avatar in our virtual mirror. What will they do? Wave back at you? Dance? Play?

artists: See Virror by LumimaLabPDX at Big Pink
metadata.description: Virror is not the art. Virror is the medium on which you, the Art, play. A stark brutalist monolith with minimal aesthetic, your portal into the digital dimension. On the other side is a new version of you, one made of light and energy. Virror 2.0 is the expansion of an interactive piece created in 2017. The previous works from our art collective were beautiful and engaging to look at, but none were interactive. Virror was created to fill the gap. Nothing much to look at, all interaction.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: I think art is the conduit between living things.
place_info.place_address: 431 SW Harvey Milk St Portland Oregon 97204

metadata.description: This project began with a simple idea:Get people to stop looking down — and start looking up.We spend so much time buried in our phones, trapped by tech that was meant to connect us but often isolates us instead. In doing so, we miss everything around us: the architecture, the flowers, the colors, the people, the possibility of connection.I wanted to interrupt that downward gaze — to give people a reason to pause, look up, and see the beauty that’s already here.Take a breath. Look around. Things aren’t as bad as they seem.It’s all perspective.Thank you to Downtown Clean & Safe for supporting this project.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Someone said, The eye is the window to the soul. As a person who doesn't believe in souls, I think the eyes are a conduit between living things. A sup
place_info.place_address: 320 SW Harvey Milk St. Portland Oregon 97204

In doing so, we miss everything around us: the architecture, the flowers, the colors, the people, the possibility of connection.I wanted to interrupt that downward gaze — to give people a reason to pause, look up, and see the beauty that’s already here.Take a breath. place_info.place_editorial_summary: Tesseract (Study) is a compact mirrored sculpture representing a fourth-dimensional cube expressed within three-dimensional space. Through layered ref
place_info.place_address: 320 SW Harvey Milk St Portland Oregon 97204

metadata.description: Tesseract (Study) is a compact mirrored light sculpture representing a fourth-dimensional cube translated into three-dimensional space. Drawing from mathematical theory and sacred geometry, the work uses reflective surfaces and precisely programmed LED light to generate the illusion of infinite depth and recursive form within a contained volume.Though modest in scale, the piece functions as a perceptual portal. As light reflects endlessly within the mirrored interior, the boundaries between interior and exterior, object and observer, begin to dissolve. The sculpture invites close viewing and slow contemplation, rewarding subtle shifts in perspective with continuously evolving visual complexity.

place_info.place_name: Life Imitates Art: The Secret Life of Plants
place_info.place_editorial_summary: This installation celebrates the resilience of plants through constant, sometimes imperceptible motions.
place_info.place_address: 426 SW Harvey Milk St, Portland Oregon 97204

metadata.description: Spores drift. Insects stir. Carbon is sequestered. Oxygen is produced. Photons are synthesized. Veins glow and shrivel. Rain returns. Growth is a give and take. It asks us to embrace resilience not as a singular act but as a series of events built through many small, ongoing actions, even dormancy—rest.The animation turns biological processes into choreography, blurring the line between ecology and art—life imitating art, and art revealing life.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A pair of curved steel pipes lined with a series of concentric rings internally illuminated. Purpose/Vision: put a Quirk in your smile 😉
Treasure chests with treasure for the taking…if you dare! (Really, we dare you. I mean you don't really need all of your limbs, do you?)

metadata.description: Beneath the glowing waves, a colossal crab guards its glittering hoard. They call it The Keeper, a creature older than the sea itself. Scattered around it lie enchanted treasure chests, each whispering secrets from strange worlds—some glow, some tremble, some seem almost alive. In the dark beyond, eerie shapes shift and watch, half-hidden in the murk. Among the coral lie eleven massive gold coins, said to mark each interdimensional ship the crab has dragged down from the skies – 11 of them to be exact. Count them all if you dare—but beware: the Keeper sees everything, and it doesn’t like to share.

place_info.place_name: A Whale of a Good Time
place_info.place_editorial_summary: A sunken ship. A cosmic whale. Mysterious alien text. What does it all mean? View inside this strange world and see for yourself!

artists: A Whale of a Good Time at 520 SW 4th
metadata.description: Beneath the shimmering surface, a giant cosmic whale glides through the ruins of a forgotten world. The locals call it the Oh Whale—ancient, alien, impossibly alive. Below, the sunken SS Fathom rests among coral, where tiny Meemers crawl from a cracked porthole, collecting shiny scraps and chattering in curious clicks. Nearby, a glowing portal pulses in the reef, warping light and color as if it was from a dream. Look closely and you’ll find alien letters all about. What do they say?! Legend had it that if you find them all and unscramble them, you will be let in on one of the deepest mysteries of the deep! (SO deep!)

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Peer into the tiny world of out of this world diatoms!

metadata.description: Peer into three giant magnifying glasses that are daring you to lean in and take a look. What you find on the other side is not a textbook science moment—it’s a full-on microscopic party. A wild world of diatoms explodes into view, glowing under UV light like they just crawled out of another dimension and immediately decided to start a dance club.These tiny weirdos spin, hang, wobble, and swarm with chaotic joy, as if gravity is more of a suggestion than a rule. They are strange, glowing, and delightfully alive—proof that even the smallest worlds are absolutely unhinged.These diatoms were created by the artists from Roboto Octopodo – the same crew that brought you the immersive art experiences FATHOM and PRISM.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A point of view- a way of seeing something. We each see things slightly differently because we interpret what we see from our own experience.

metadata.description: I capture details of the world around me, things that are often overlooked. I magnify them and layer them and try to present them in a different way. I shoot everything on my iPhone (or digital camera) and edit in Adobe Premiere. None of these images are computer generated- it is all in the shooting and layering.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: These Sputnik Urchins have been envisioned as interstellar travellers that use patterns of light for communication.

metadata.description: These Sputnik Urchins have been envisioned as interstellar travelers that use patterns of light for communication. They visit Earth to commune with their tiny benthic siblings — armored creatures covered in spines, with a symmetry and texture that evoke wonder. They reveal nature’s intricate design on a miniature scale. Existing amid vast forces such as changing tides and marine ecosystems, they symbolize hidden strength and unexpected elegance.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Vignettes of tiny worlds, glimpses of imagined lives, snapshots of micro spaces, preserved in time under protective cases

metadata.description: Tiny enchanted worlds glow inside an old apothecary, where a curious creature slips between dusty bottles – mischievously exploring the secrets in the shadows.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A light "blender" contorts, twists, minces, chops, and dices projected light, filling a room like a smoothie.

metadata.description: Think of this as a prism: a small piece of glass that can break an incoming light ray into a spread of unanticipated information. Using many computer controlled servo motors, multiple hanging reflective mylar strips are twisted and redirected in varying rhythms. Small panels of incoming projected light, now reflected in all directions, fill the walls, floor, ceiling, and windows of the entire space with kinetic light in a three dimensional collage. We hope to incorporate an interactive opportunity for viewers to use their cell phone flashlight to temporarily control the behavior of the machine.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Everything in this exhibition is made from newspaper and plastic collected from the streets of Portland!

metadata.description: The inspiration for this installation is a visual critique to a continuous environmental catastrophe which has been occurring off the Southeast U.S. coast since the early seventies. The State of Florida attempted to replace coral reefs that had been swept away due to the increased dragnet fishing over the years. They introduced thousands of used rubber tires into the ecosystem, hoping that the local flora and fauna would interact with it in the same manner as the coral. Equal to the area of thirty-one football fields, the nylon and steel bands holding the tires together almost immediately broken loose, washing the rubber tires out to sea. Massive portions of these tires continue to collide with the remaining coral devastating the ecosystem.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: The Mini Drive-In Theater is a piece that plays with shifts in scale to invite a viewer to explore a miniature scene and evoke subjective narratives.

metadata.description: Enter the scene of “The Mini Drive-In Theater”, where things are not always as they seem. At first glance, it may appear to simply be a miniature version of a drive-in theater. Although, upon further exploration, a viewer will discover a variety of components that will reveal mysterious narratives. The imagery projected on the small screen present yet another level of wonder. What movie are all of these characters gathered to see? What curious dimension are we peering into?

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Suspended upcycle plastics (burbled, baked, embellished), an abstract field of effervescent repurposed sameness, 1 bedazzled siren

artists: Teresa (Tre) Roberts, Little Witch Studio PDX
metadata.description: Little Witch Studio (a project of Present Time Dream Factory) embraces and expresses neurodiversity through art and community. We’re all just stemming here. Tre happens to be stemming with upcycled and repurposed objects sourced locally from the streets and thrift stores, supplemented with office and art supplies. Beads come from broken jewelry, the little things you take for granted are elevated and showcased, from buttons to rubber bands. Light infuses and informs it all with a surreal effervescent quality.Next time before you throw away that #6 plastic container, cut out a few shapes, color them with sharpies & bake ’em at 325 degrees. It’s not upcycled til the art fairies sing.At Little Witch Studio, we make stuff and nonsense.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: All origami blackligjht responsive arachnid/insect installation. A rather large paper spider on a large we with a variety of other origami insects

metadata.description: This installation will be completely black light responsive. The webbing will take up approximately 6 square feet behind glass. The display will be focused around a large origami spider and feature a multitude of other origami insects trapped in the webbing. The day and night difference in the models will exemplify art in the tiniest way.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Infinite + Unity = Infunity A mesmerizing moiré kinetic sculpture where your movements sculpt the patterns that emerge from light and shadow.

metadata.description: Infunity Gate is an illusory, shifting sculpture that bends and distorts shapes using simple, layered patterns that interact in surprisingly complex ways. Placing your appendages inside the control nodes allows you to influence the speed and direction of these morphing patterns. Gentle backlighting casts an ethereal glow, accentuating the sculpture’s movement and creating an otherworldly ambiance. The installation’s slow rotation invites you to become lost in the undulating patterns that emerge. Gate explores the interplay of perspective, light, and shadow; a theme that pervades all Infunity’s artwork.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Someone said, The eye is the window to the soul. As someone who doesn't believe in souls, I think eyes are a conduit between living things.

Look around. Things aren’t as bad as they seem.It’s all perspective.Thank you to Downtown Clean & Safe for supporting this project.
