Things aren’t as bad as they seem.It’s all perspective.Thank you to Downtown Clean & Safe for supporting this project.And deep thanks to Melvin Mark for turning off the lights on your buildings — giving us the canvas to light up the night.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Reflected, shifting color turns light aquatic and celestial, inviting people to slow and watch fleeting moments drift and quietly converge.

metadata.description: Tidal Breath transforms the window bays into a luminous surface that appears to breathe through shifting light, weaving pulses, particles, and reflections into a calm, tidal rhythm. A slow inhale brightens the façade as currents of light gather and shimmer, drifting toward moments of radiant convergence. Mirrors and dichroic accents scatter glints across the architecture, echoing sunlight on moving water. As the exhale unfolds, the brightness softens and the currents release, dispersing into a quiet shimmer. Inspired by rivers, tides, and atmospheric motion, the work invites people to pause and reflect.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Love and Light features three panels (heart, sun, eye) made from fused glass murrini. Images are framed by color changing light creating a color dance

metadata.description: This piece is about joy, beauty, light, positivity, and the ability to embrace those traits in ourselves and others. Originally intended to be viewed from both sides, the clear spaces are a place to make silly faces, smile and be seen by friends. Love and Light intends to create a space for people to contemplate gratitude and hope for the future while enjoying something fun and a little trippy.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A pair of glowing flowers that shift colors with your touch, translating tiny electrical changes into playful, reactive light.
place_info.place_address: 1132 SW Harvey Milk St #104 Portland Oregon 97205

artists: Aura Flora by Luke Michaels at World Trade Center
metadata.description: Aura Flora invites you to become part of its circuitry. Each flower listens to subtle variations in your skin’s conductivity and responds with evolving light patterns unique to your interaction. These bio-reactive blooms create a gentle dialogue between human presence and illuminated sculpture, turning invisible signals into a living display of motion and color.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Peer into the kaleidoscopic portal for a glimmer of what lies within Portland's smallest park!

metadata.description: Originally designed and installed at Mill Ends Park for the 2025 Portland Winter Light Festival, Chasing Rainbows shines a light on 80 years of miniature mischief.In 1946, journalist Dick Fagan gazed out his office window and envisioned a leprechaun colony inhabiting a hole meant for a utility pole. His whimsy sparked a newspaper column, a Portland Park designation, a Guinness World Record and decades of lore. Despite its humble footprint (452 square inches!), the legend of Mill Ends Park continues to grow year after year.Now located at The Sentinel, Chasing Rainbows provides a portal to Mill Ends Park. Peer in and transport yourself! There is an infinite treasure within, hidden in plain sight. Can you see the unseeable?

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Explore downtown Portland through a scavenger hunt that reveals the pivotal LGBTQ moments and places woven into the city’s history.

metadata.description: The Portland Gayborhood project brings LGBTQ+ history to life with illuminated light boxes placed at key downtown sites, forming a scavenger-hunt pathway between Pride Plaza and the future Darcelle XV Plaza. Each light box marks a building where an LGBTQ+ space once existed and features archival photos, news clippings, and brief narratives sourced from community members and the Umbrella Project. QR codes lead visitors to the next stop, creating a self-guided journey through the people, places, and culture that shaped Portland’s LGBTQ+ community.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Jesus used the mustard seed to illustrate how tiny things can grow into something great. If he told that story here, we think he’d use the Doug Fir.

metadata.description: Jesus said: “The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed, which a man took and planted in his field. Though it is the smallest of all seeds, yet when it grows, it is the largest of garden plants and becomes a tree, so that the birds come and perch in its branches.”The plant Jesus was talking about was around 15 feet tall, impressive in a desert environment. But if he was in Oregon, we imagine him holding a distinctive cone from a Doug Fir, gesturing to a towering 300-footer, hundreds of years old – a testament to strength and endurance. In tough times like these knowing our small efforts to help feed the hungry, shelter the houseless and comfort the distressed stand as a lasting testament to what we can do if we stick together.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Welcome to a world of mycelial communication networks. Join your fellow travelers on a journey to communication, creation and wonder.
place_info.place_address: Corner of SW 10th & SW Alder St Portland Oregon 97205

metadata.description: The Northern Sea Otter is a “Keystone Species” that helps the Giant Kelp grow and create a thriving ecosystem off the coast of the Western United States. The Otters do this by eating Sea Urchins. Without them, the urchins consume the kelp and create underwater deserts called “Urchin Barrens”. Come move your body and guide the Otters to a tasty snack!

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Nautilus Deep Sea casts prismatic patterns of shadow and light on a larger-than-life scale, evoking a sense of reverence for the mysterious deep sea.

metadata.description: The same mathematical patterns that shape distant galaxies and govern the orbits of planets and moons appear in the spiral of the Nautilus. Dwelling at near-seafloor depths of 1,800 feet, the Nautilus is an ancient marine mollusk whose shell consists of individual pressure-resistant chambers which grow continuously outward in a logarithmic spiral over its lifetime.The Nautilus has become listed under the Endangered Species Act due to overharvesting for its shell.The deep sea occupies over 95% of Earth’s surface! As stewards of the Indo-Pacific off the west coast, viewers are invited to contemplate a sense of interconnection with even the most mysterious and distant beings on our precious planet.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: An illuminated stainless steel sculpture inspired by spider mythology, honoring femme resilience and the unseen labor that supports community.

artists: Nora Hughes of Magic Marker Design
metadata.description: Spider Temple consists of eight freestanding stainless steel forms, shaped like spider legs and radiating from a shared center. Each leg is illuminated from within; light spilling through intricate laser-cut designs that cast shifting shadows and woven patterns of light. The piece draws inspiration from a 3,200 year old mural of a spider deity in northern Peru’s Virú province. Spiders have long been mythological symbols of elemental balance, fire and water, and the non-binary complexities of femme identity.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Metal and Light Sculpture by Hap Tivey

metadata.description: Hap Tivey’s site-specific art installation for Southpark Seafood is one of many audience-engaging light installations he has done over the past several decades. Tivey’s immersive light sculptures are included in numerous prestigious private and public collections such as the Museum of Modern Art in New York City, the Menil Collection in Houston, and the Guggenheim Museums – both in New York City and Bilbao, Spain.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Projection mapped multi-layered lasercut wood art. In three different intricate gothic window panels, I tell the story of how a star in born.

metadata.description: Each gothic window lets you peer into the moment a star is born. A fantastical story of a tiny woodland sprite who’s destined to join her sisters and brothers in the sky.This installation is told through layered, lasercut wood. Each panel carved to create depth, shadow, and shifting perspective. Hand-painted surfaces catch projected light, animating roots, leaves, and paths as the story unfolds. Projection mapping gently helps the layers breath and form, transforming solid wood into living light and guiding viewers from forest hollow to open sky. Through cut, color, and illumination, the piece explores transformation. From a tiny little light on the earthy floor to something large and eternal.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Illoominated 2 weaves threads of laser light in a tribute to the first programmer, Ada Lovelace, and the hidden connection between looms and computers

metadata.description: What would a divine machine that weaves more than a tapestry, but perhaps the fabric of creation, look like? The concept of Illoominated 2 combines the Simulation Hypothesis, which suggests reality exists because it’s being calculated on a higher plane of existence, with the Universal Turing Machine, a hypothetical model of a computer that can simulate any conceivable program.In the early 1800s the lineage of the computer advanced significantly with the patent of the Jacquard loom, a machine that mechanically stored data and used punch cards to program various patterns. However, the true potential of the computer wasn’t realized until the groundbreaking work of the brilliant Ada Lovelace, who conceived of the first algorithms.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Someone said, The eye is the window to the soul. As someone who doesn't believe in souls, I think eyes are a conduit between living things.

metadata.description: This project began with a simple idea: Get people to stop looking down — and start looking up.We spend so much time buried in our phones, trapped by tech that was meant to connect us but often isolates us instead. In doing so, we miss everything around us: the architecture, the flowers, the colors, the people, the possibility of connection.I wanted to interrupt that downward gaze — to give people a reason to pause, look up, and see the beauty that’s already here.Take a breath. Look around. Things aren’t as bad as they seem.It’s all perspective.Thank you to Downtown Clean & Safe for supporting this project.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Pulse brings the hidden underground network connecting these trees to life with vivid visualizations of light.
place_info.place_address: SW Park & SW Madison St Portland Oregon 97201

metadata.description: Pulse is an exploration of our connection to nature, often overlooked as we pass through it every day. Deep underground, the trees create a network with their roots, allowing them to share nutrients, energy, and information with each other. They can sense things happening near them and share it with the other trees in the forest.Pulse brings this hidden network to life by suspending a large LED ring around each tree, allowing the tree to express its current state in real time through vibrant visualizations of light.You are invited to sit back and watch the forest create stunning visualizations of light, or walk through the forest, immersing yourself amongst the trees, and discover how the forest reacts to your presence.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: "Harriet Tubman" hums with the glow of neon pink, forming the word “Tenderhead”– the central concept and title for Lisa Jarrett’s PAM exhibition.

metadata.description: In the exhibition:“Tenderhead”, Lisa Jarrett weaves together her interest in migration and tracing lost familial histories to Beauty Supply stores and salons—critical intersections of Black life.“Harriet Tubman” hums with the glow of neon pink, forming the word “Tenderhead,” the exhibition’s central concept and title. “Tenderhead” recalls a question many remember from childhood hair care: “Are you tenderheaded?” The work links this sensitivity with Tubman, a widely written-about historical figure whose depth and complexities remain unknowable, inviting reflection on the tender and vulnerable facets of Black women’s labor.“Tenderhead” appears in PAM’s new Black Art & Experiences galleries, visible to all who walk the community passageway.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Experience the radiant glow of Ugo Rondinone’s "the sun", a new 18-foot sculpture illuminating the Portland Art Museum’s west courtyard at night.

metadata.description: Ugo Rondinone’s “the sun”, is a striking new landmark for Portland. Rising over 18 feet in the Portland Art Museum’s west courtyard, this luminous ring of tree branches in gilded bronze transforms natural shapes into a glowing sculpture that shines brilliantly after dark. Inspired by the rhythms of nature and the passage of time, “the sun” becomes a beacon at night, illuminating the glass facade of the new Mark Rothko Pavilion. Although the Museum is closed during the evening festival hours, we invite you to bask in “the sun” from the courtyard and walkways, and to return during regular hours to explore the transformed Museum.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A handmade steel tree sculpture with a glowing video projection canopy.

metadata.description: A sequel to last year’s “Utopia”, our new version for 2026 is “Porch/Light”. Connecting with the theme of “All the Little Things”, our existing tree sculpture will be enhanced with flickering lightning bugs, sound by Callie Day, and projected video elements by microscopist James Weiss.Lessons from our first year taught us that the tree canopy can be further developed as layered fabric segments, more robust for wind, and that the ground and surrounding area can be included as projection surfaces. (A lesson of children playing in the projected light during installation). This project is in honor of my mother, her sisters, and all of their mothers.

place_info.place_name: Free Hot Cocoa at Forest Hospitality Tent at PSU Park Blocks
place_info.place_editorial_summary: A glowing tent in the illuminated park blocks serving free cocoa by hosts in original light costumes.

metadata.description: Add to your Light Festival look with free face painting provided in the tent on Fridays and Saturdays from 6 to 8 PM.PSU Hosted by LAIKA

place_info.place_editorial_summary: The often dark windows of the Victorian Simon Benson House will be illuminated with behind-the-scenes images and video from LAIKA.
place_info.place_address: 1803 SW Park Avenue Park Ave. Portland OR

artists: Images and footage provided by LAIKA Studios
Projection coordination/mapping and additional animation by Stephen Lee, Associate Professor, Portland State University
metadata.description: The glowing pink house, reminiscent of the Pink Palace in LAIKA’s Coraline (2009), offers a special view behind-the-scenes of the film’s groundbreaking stop-motion animation process.

place_info.place_name: Windows into stop motion magic… a look inside LAIKA studios
place_info.place_editorial_summary: Multi-Image panorama of the hand of the artist at work, inside LAIKA Studios, as weaved into Smith Memorial Student Union at PSU
The normally drab winter character of the park block trees are animated with splashes of changing color.

metadata.description: The light installation will not only transform the trees, but will cast beautiful shadows on the ground below.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: The projection-mapped visuals for this piece were created with code by students in DES 343: Creative Coding at Portland State University.

metadata.description: DES 343 is a studio course exploring code-based approaches for creative projects including generative design and interactivity. Students will work with contemporary programming languages and tools, which may rotate from term to term. No previous coding experience is required.This class was taught by Stephen Lee and includes work by Andy Aldrich, Ezra Chiou, Owen Cook, Elissa El-Tayib, Adilene Galindo Lopez, Patrick Hart, Maggie Huang, Rocky Li, Meredith Padilla, Connor Tagliaferri, Heidi Tong, Jasmine Torres, Rei Tran, Abby Turk, Blossom Vue, Jennifer Wachner, and Karla Walker.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Visible through full height windows a sculpture of paper, light, and projected images will reference the history of the building.

metadata.description: In reference to the theme of “All the Little Things” we take inspiration from our past and current small stories of everyday life. “Below the Fold” references the stories that appear in newspapers, not as giant headlines, but further down the page. It’s really these smaller stories, continually being told day in and day out, that make up the larger story of our lives. As the building where the Oregonian newspaper was printed between 1948 to 1974, the space played a role in bringing these stories to our community.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: This piece reimagines the mural Peace N Love through warm light, exploring calm, connection, and shared presence within a living space.

metadata.description: Peace in the Glow transforms the language of an abstract mural into a living field of light. Thousands of warm LEDs trace organic, interconnected forms inspired by the original Peace N Love design, allowing illumination to replace pigment and space to replace canvas. As day shifts into evening, the installation gently emerges—creating a quiet visual rhythm that feels both intimate and expansive. Viewers are invited to slow down, gather, and experience peace not as a symbol, but as a shared atmosphere of unity, calm, and human connection.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Las Estrellas shine with flickering and sparkling light like stars in the night sky.

metadata.description: Las Estrellas was inspired by the traditional Mexican pinatas that feature three or seven-pointed stars and found hanging above places people gather at the winter holidays. These iconic shapes are blended with the forms found in the colorful southern Italian lighting displays called Luminaries. Both archetypal symbols of their regions celebrate the spirit of festivity, joy and community.This year’s PDXWLF theme asks how can one memory set you on a path to the future? Our response is – Las Estrellas.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Millions of microscopic points coordinate and coalesce into macroscopic pattern and form.
place_info.place_address: SW Columia at SW 2nd Avenue Portland Oregon 97201

metadata.description: Millions of particles swarm, drift, and respond to your presence. Using real-time computer vision, the installation detects viewers through the window and incorporates them into the system—faces emerge from the chaos, gestures scatter or attract the swarm, bodies become nodes in a living digital network. Drawing on simulations of collective behavior and point cloud reconstructions of Portland spaces, Micro Macro explores how pattern and meaning arise from aggregation. How many stars make a constellation? How many trees make a forest? How many pixels make an image?

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A first-time collaboration between artists Saleam and Todd Moyer bends reality as it mixes physical with digital.

artists: Windows of The Mind at Collumbia Square
metadata.description: Windows of the Mind is a collaborative piece that portrays raw creative energy becoming physical, and represents the transformation that takes place when artists come together, building upon each others’ work. Saleam is renowned for his murals and abstract stylings, while Todd brings a knack for lighting and technology.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: The ancient art of Sacred Geometry meets modern Light.
Kaleidoscopic imagery projects across layers of translucent fabric, creating a luminous holographic field of depth and motion.

metadata.description: This installation invites viewers into a space where light becomes a living architecture and perception stretches between scales. Multiple layers of translucent fabric are illuminated with changing patterns, allowing the imagery to refract, diffuse, and drift through parallax. The layered screen behaves like a breathing membrane, responding to every shift in pattern and color.Alongside this dimensional projection, two small terrariums act as earthly counterpoints. Within them, plants and insects cast unexpectedly large, colorful shadows, grounding the experience in the tangible and biological. The piece gestures toward the shared architecture of scales, reminding us that the scaffolding of galaxies is also in “all the little things”.ON VIEW & FOR SALE You can find select works by this artist for sale on consignment at official Portland Winter Light Festival merch booths at select anchor locations during the Festival.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A four-foot paper octopus drifts among sea stars and urchins, its form alive with subtle movement, patterns, and the quiet magic of small things.

metadata.description: Samyra is a four-foot octopus, crafted with LEDs and delicate forms, resting among sea stars and urchin-like shapes. Her fluid, flowing presence evokes tides, subtle movement, and the quiet intelligence of her kind. Patterns shift across her form, revealing the beauty of countless small pieces coming together to create something greater than the sum of their parts. A study in emergence and delicate motion, Samyra invites you to linger, notice, and marvel at the fragile magic of the little things.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A visual essay on relative perspective, or 'big things that appear small and small things that appear big, and other stuff'.

metadata.description: Our perspective shapes how we see and understand our world, and each of us has a different perspective, based on our personal history and the perspectives of those who are close to us. For years I photographed things that are often overlooked- shadows, reflections, juxtapositions of textures and forms (detritus in the tideline of a beach, stains and cracks in concrete, or the scraped side of a barge). When framed in a particular way, these can be compelling images.For several years now I have been working in video, shooting images on my iPhone and layering and processing the images into moving tapestries of light and form.

place_info.place_name: Illuminated Works, An Exhibit By The Pacific Northwest Sculptors
place_info.place_editorial_summary: An installation of illuminated sculptures from a diverse group of artists who utilize light as a sculptural medium in their artworks.
place_info.place_address: SW Madison between SW 2nd and 3rd Ave Portland Oregon 97204

metadata.description: The Illuminated Works exhibit is a selection of light based sculptures by several members of the Pacific Northwest Sculptors, a non-profit organization that helps sculptors thrive through inspiration, collaboration and education. This show will include Craig Dorety’s mathematical LED space-time distortions, Torrey Dasmann’s tiny glowing houses, Jessica Stroia’s mysterious illuminated mirrors, and Bob Deasy’s glowing abstract stone sculpture, among others. These works will be displayed in the Justice Center Sidewalk Gallery throughout the month of February 2026.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Traditional neon pieces that reflect glimpses of the illuminated street life of Japan.

metadata.description: Inspired by the imagery of the streets of Japan, Perry’s neon work captures moments of the visual experience of Japan’s urban landscape. These moments consist of a construction site’s flashing warning sign, the graphic of a tobacco stand, and signs left over from other times that still hold the same messages for generations.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: 23pdx will be a glow with wall projections to enhance our glowing clam and mermaid selfie station. A magical world under the sea.

metadata.description: The intent is to turn the gallery space at 23 NW 4th Ave, into an under the sea experience, where folks will be able to come and take selfies with The Clam and Mermaid Station during the Festival and experience the live projections happening inside to enhance the space.Over the course of the weekend, we would like to entertain the crowds and offer up a fun family friendly experience.Sunnyside Environmental School elementary students were invited to explore the theme “All the Little Things” by designing imaginative bugs that conceal a symbol of their identity. Each piece was created with UV neon paint pens on clear acetate and comes to life when illuminated under black light.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Using aomori nebuta techniques, wire and paper forms glow from within, honoring Old Town’s cultural roots and cultivating community through light.

metadata.description: This light-based window installation celebrates the small, often-overlooked moments cultivated at p:ear through creative mentorship. Using light and sculptural elements, the work brings visibility to quiet acts of care, creativity, and connection that support Portland youth as they grow and thrive. Through education, art, recreation, and job training, p:ear builds trusting relationships with homeless and marginalized youth, affirming personal worth and possibility.Created collaboratively by p:ear youth, staff, and volunteers, the installation reflects how simple gestures, showing up, listening, and creating together, can ripple outward into lasting personal and community change, transforming care into a visible source of light.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A group exhibition of light-based artworks by Dan Flavin, Peter Gronquist, Fabiola Menchelli, Gregg Renfrow and Hap Tivey.

metadata.description: To commence Elizabeth Leach Gallery’s 45th anniversary year, we are pleased to present Sculpted Light. Bringing together works by Hap Tivey, Dan Flavin, Peter Gronquist, Fabiola Menchelli and Gregg Renfrow, the exhibition will explore the legacy of the Light and Space movement that originated in Southern California in the 1970s. Gallery founder, Elizabeth Leach, became well acquainted with the movement’s artists during her studies in Claremont, California, and exhibited work by Tivey during the gallery’s first year.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Enormous dahlias intertwined in steel and light.

metadata.description: This piece is made with connection and care for one another in mind. Inspired by my parents celebrating their 50th wedding anniversary before my father passed. Come enjoy the dazzling light display!

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Lost or not, The Grove beckons! Let your curiosity ponder mandalas and sacred geometry as the lamps draw you toward your hidden side within.
place_info.place_address: 2025 NW Northrup St, Portland, OR 97209 Portland Oregon 97209

metadata.description: Winter’s Guiding Grove is a futuristic vision of harmonious cohabitation, where nature, technology, and the vibrant spirit of Portland converge. This year, the Portland pride is enriched through a collaboration with local artist Ryan Kerrigan for an immersive experience of hand drawn mandala artwork featured atop the lamps.These enchanting lanterns, meticulously laser-cut from wood and illuminated by dancing addressable LED lights, cast mesmerizing shadows of interconnected leaves and geometric patterns. They not only guide curious onlookers through the festival but also invite introspection and a deeper connection between mind and spirit.ON VIEW & FOR SALE You can find select works by this artist for sale on consignment at official Portland Winter Light Festival merch booths at select anchor locations during the Festival.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A music-driven light installation transforms our glass-front gallery into a glowing beacon, with shifting color and sound spilling onto the street.

metadata.description: This installation explores the relationship between light, sound, and public space, transforming a glass-front gallery into a luminous presence within the city. Color-changing light sequences flow through the interior, visible through the gallery façade and casting light onto the sidewalk and street beyond. The lighting shifts are paired with music, creating an ambient experience that unfolds over time.This project is powered by Lutron Ketra, whose advanced lighting technology enables fluid, dynamic color transitions, and was realized in collaboration with Mari Design, our installation partner.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: "Books Are Lit!" celebrates the illuminating power of reading as selected book covers are recreated and lit from within.

artists: Lincoln High School Production Design and Construction students
metadata.description: Lincoln High School students in Joseph Hewitt’s Product Design and Construction classes chose books selected by their teacher-librarian, Lori Lieberman, to recreate and illuminate. They’ve created a magical and ethereal library where literature literally glows from within.The installation is inside the beautiful, new Lincoln High School Library.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: PNCA's building will light up with spectacular projections of monumental artworks covering the building’s surfaces!

metadata.description: To celebrate the Portland Winter Light Festival, PNCA’s building will light up with spectacular projections of monumental artworks covering the building’s surfaces! This dynamic transformation turns the structure into a living, breathing work of art, glowing with creativity and innovation. The projections are a series of artworks and animations created by PNCA students & faculty and offer a glimpse into the imaginative minds of PNCA’s community, inviting onlookers to experience art in a bold and accessible way.The PNCA Projection Project will be on view during the First Thursday Art Walk in the Pearl District on Thursday 2/5, 5-8pm and on Opening Night of the Portland Winter Light Festival on Friday 2/6, 5-8pm! Free & open to all!RSVPHERESpecial Guest –Join Dance at Play: The Art Bus Experience

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Animated visions of Portland transformed into a glowing dreamscape, where streets, bridges, and neighborhoods take shape in flowing light and color.

metadata.description: Portland Aglow is a series of animated cityscapes that reimagine Portland as a luminous environment shaped by light, motion, and atmosphere. Using stylized animation and generative techniques, familiar bridges, streets, and buildings take shape as flowing forms that feel both cosmic and grounded in place. Rather than depicting the city as it exists today, the work explores how Portland might feel when viewed through imagination and possibility, inviting viewers to slow down and experience a calm, glowing version of the city.

place_info.place_name: Portland Revels Celebrates LIGHT! at Director Park
place_info.place_editorial_summary: Portland Revellers celebrate the "return of the light" in winter, through songs, dances, skits and comedy that drive the dark away.

metadata.description: The Portland Revels performing arts organization celebrates the seasons through song, dance, story and ritual of the past and present, creating community across generations and cultures.At the 2026 Portland Winter Lights Festival, small groups of Revellers will share short musical performances that showcase how a variety of cultures and traditions celebrate the lengthening days after the longest night of the Winter Solstice . . . the literal and metaphorical “return of the light”.Schedule for 2/66:30 – Renegade Rose Morris Dancers7:00 – Musicians of the Court7:30 – Renegade Rose Morris Dancers7:50 – Musicians of the Court8:15 – All – Grand Finale

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Come explore insects, fungus, and even crystals that make way for decay in a black light display–a neon celebration of the cycle of death and life!

metadata.description: Beauchene Decay is inspired from the famous anatomy model, the Beauchene skull. Much like the the exploded, disarticulated nature of the model, glowing beetles and shimmering flies are suspended around our studio assistant skeleton while colorful patterns of grubs, fungus, and bacteria bring it all into frame.While is it easy to notice the macro systems that make up our day -to -day routine, its important to know there are elaborate systems in the micro that make the macro possible.This window display is designed and assembled by JD Cox of Grave Raiment Studio. The print studio opened in downtown in July 2025 as a retail and creative space featuring work of local artists as well as hosting classes, workshops and pop up events.*See IG link@graveraimentstudiofor a list of classes and workshops during the Festival.

place_info.place_name: Portland Sleestak Loves Portland Winter Light Festival
place_info.place_editorial_summary: No longer in the parallel universe of Land of the Lost, our Portland Sleestak is lighting up The Sleestak Factory big for this special event!

metadata.description: No longer in the parallel universe of Land of the Lost, so primitive and LED free, our Portland Sleestak is lighting up the Sleestak Factory for this special event!!!The Portland Sleestak delights in the wonder and joy of light! The Portland Winter Light Festival has become one of his favorite times of the year!The Sleestak Factory is where artist and creator Brent Marr hand builds his incredible Sleestak suits. It alone is a place of wonder, but during the Portland Winter Light Fest it will glow with unheralded glory!The Sleestak will be out and about during the Portland Winter Light Festival to take photos with all the wonderful people attending the festival. Stop by the Factory at 617 SW 4th Ave.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Dodecahedron Kaleidoscope is an infinity mirror sculpture based on the geometry of the twelve-sided Platonic solid. Through mirrored facets and carefu

metadata.description: Dodecahedron Kaleidoscope is an infinity mirror light sculpture derived from the geometry of the dodecahedron, a twelve-sided Platonic solid long associated with harmony, proportion, and the underlying structure of space. Each mirrored face acts as both boundary and portal, fragmenting light into a continuously unfolding kaleidoscopic field that appears to extend infinitely beyond the physical object.As viewers look around the sculpture, subtle changes in angle and proximity produce shifting patterns of symmetry and depth. Precisely programmed LED light animates the interior, revealing repeating geometries that oscillate between order and complexity. The result is an immersive visual experience contained within a finite form.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Snippets of memory, made up places, thoughts, dreams or destinations. A chosen 4, not in any particular order and not to scale.

metadata.description: The snippets are pieces of random scenes that popped to mind. Not a storyline, more of an impression. Not following order but a what if. Materials are repurposed scrap, then reformed into identifiable objects in a made up scene.Thank you: Ellumiglow for the lighting support, Kay for always supporting community, Misty Post for making the stained glass raindrops, Olivier Bouwman for the heart template, Tad Cody for the disco planet support/inspiration.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Where sparkles meet purrs to create a meowgical land of cats and kittens, shimmering with love and lighting up your heart with glittering joy.

metadata.description: This year, GiftyKitty transforms into a shimmering cat wonderland. Over 100 cat-head mirror balls cast dancing reflections through a blacklight-lit space, while soft purrs and smooth beats create a cozy feline ambiance. A dozen GiftyKitty artists contribute sparkling, reflective, and glow-in-the-dark works celebrating the tiny wonders that shine the brightest; light, memory, love, and the meowgic of “All the Little Things.” Spurrkland invites visitors to rediscover the small moments that make life glow.* Find all participating artists on GiftyKitty/Spurrkland web page

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A colorful array of the classic Valentine’s Day candies with their heartfelt expressions in traditional neon light!

artists: Perry Pfister – The Tiny Spoon Neon Signs
metadata.description: There is something magical about sharing love and affection on Valentine’s Day. We use candies and cards to be a little more vulnerable with our feelings. This work is meant to reflect those little bits of love and vulnerability that the sweethearts help us express. The sweethearts are made in a variety of colors of traditional neon, which is glass tubing and neon or argon gas electrified!

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Vibrant sunrise and sunset paintings with hidden LED lights that light up the horizon

metadata.description: These seven acrylic paintings on wood panels are a collaboration between Catherine and her husband. Catherine paints the sky on a “background” wood panel and the foreground is painted on a separate panel cut along the horizon by Catherine’s husband. He installs a strip of LED lights between the two panels and mounts the panels together. The paintings look great and “normal” when the lights are off but they are next-level when you turn them on!Each painting is controlled by a remote with multiple light colors and settings available. The paintings are framed in a maple floater frame by a Portland framer. The frames are 2″ deep, creating a shadowbox effect with the lights illuminating the interior of the frame.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: The Love Sign is a reminder of what's really important and keeps us together.

metadata.description: The Love Sign can be seen from Cathedral Park, the St. Johns Bridge, and the Willamette River. It rests on the pier of Green Anchors, a 7-acre park that hosts a welcoming community, gardens, artists, nonprofits, and small businesses.

place_info.place_name: Oregon Convention Center Spire and Crescent Lights
place_info.place_editorial_summary: OCC's light show will provide an opportunity for its talented and creative team to showcase the center's programmable, energy efficient LED system.
place_info.place_address: 777 NE Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd Portland Oregon 97232

metadata.description: OCC’s special light show provides an opportunity for its talented and creative team to showcase the center’s programmable, energy efficient LED spire and crescent lighting system.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Art is the conduit between living things.

metadata.description: This project began with a simple idea:Get people to stop looking down — and start looking up.We spend so much time buried in our phones, trapped by tech that was meant to connect us but often isolates us instead. In doing so, we miss everything around us: the architecture, the flowers, the colors, the people, the possibility of connection.I wanted to interrupt that downward gaze — to give people a reason to pause, look up, and see the beauty that’s already here.Take a breath. Look around. Things aren’t as bad as they seem.It’s all perspective.

place_info.place_name: Glow Up at The Roll Up
place_info.place_editorial_summary: Glow Up at The Roll Up pops off at Portland Winter Lights Feb 6 to 14 with UV art, tufting classes, glowing sculptures, and big community vibes come!!
place_info.place_address: 2145 NE MLK JR BLVD Unit B Portland Oregon 97217

metadata.description: Glow Up at The Roll Up lights up the 2026 Portland Winter Light Festival February 6 to 14 with an immersive, community powered glow experience. Now in our third year, The Roll Up Artist Collective expands beyond the Black Velvet Group Art Show into hands on fun with UV tufting classes, a UV photo booth, and glowing sculptures by Matt Perez. Created with Colours PDX, Fuzzy Space, and Limelight Salon, this is not just an art show. It is a place to play, connect, and create. Step into the glow and be part of the art.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A glowing mystical mural invites you to discover a sprawling artistic city full of live painters, immersive galleries, and interactive installations.

metadata.description: A mural enchanted with inspiration beckons the public to explore the inner reaches of Portland’s most immersive art village. With an illuminated exterior mural swirling with creative life, visitors are invited to step through the mysterious veil to enter a world full of visionary art, enchanting lounges, an herbal elixir bar, and world class galleries.All who visit are sure to be transported to a realm far from the ordinary. This is a space where magic is accepted as the norm. Where dancing lights bounce off castle walls, glowing lanterns weave through underground forests, and portals of wonder await around every corner. Venture forth and discover the many creative jewels nestled within The Haven PDX.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: This piece is the 4th in an experiment of form. Though each seed is quite geometric, their curves and progression speak in an organic manner.

metadata.description: This piece consists of forty five feet tall “seed” lanterns named as such because they look akin to a fennel seed. The seeds are strung through the trees that snake through a small section of Oregon Park. This installation’s large size, movement, and simple elegance will draw together people who pass through the park into an arena of wonder and beauty, illuminating the beauty of the winter branches. The seeds create both a spectacle and a space to enter at the same time. It will be a great piece to visit during the day as well.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: An interactive play structure for young children includes 2 motion-activated swings, a glowing canopy swing, a 5ft slide, and hands-on toys.

metadata.description: Welcome to My Little Lights Playground! This illuminated play structure combines motion and light, creating a magical experience for kids. Swinging, spinning, and sliding transforms tiny lights into streams of radiance. Playtime not only fosters friendship among kids but also builds community for parents and caregivers. We are all made from stars—come play bright!The structure includes: 2 motion-activated LED swing seats, an LED-padded saucer swing canopy, a 5-foot wavy slide, and an interactive light box table with hands-on building toys.Warning: Everyone is advised to monitor their children. Play nice.Use equipment appropriately and at your own risk.Weight limit: 100lbs per swing. 500lb weight maximum for total structure.ON VIEW & FOR SALE You can find select works by this artist for sale on consignment at official Portland Winter Light Festival merch booths at select anchor locations during the Festival.

place_info.place_name: WASTE-ORATION  fragments of life, brought back into the light. Re-seen with the third eye through the chakras.
place_info.place_editorial_summary: Using discarded materials. WASTE-ORATION reveals childlike wonder in what we've overlooked urging us to slow down seeing beauty in what we cast away.

metadata.description: Waste-Oration final installation of the Waste trilogy. Wastetopia was the architecture. Based on feng shui principles. Each area glowed with chakra colors. Mapping waste as elements. It was the spiritual topography of reclamation. A city of what had been cast outs now reordered by color, energy, and vibration. WasteWoman entered. Energy current running through each Chakra she didn’t study the colors she became them. Each color a station of transformation. She was the conductor of energy channeling restoration through resonance. WASTE-ORATION after everything has settled, what remains is the single white light of childhood. Imagination curiosity wonder. We are now led back through the fragments of the past. Illuminating objects once forgot.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: 3..2..1..Blast OFF!! Space was the final frontier, but this Mechan explored it and found a mysterious space crystal.

metadata.description: Mechan 42 is our slightly unhinged space robot emissary, beamed in from a corner of the cosmos where logic is optional and awe is mandatory. Equal parts ancient sentinel and disco-era future relic, this towering metal being looks like it woke up halfway through guarding the universe and decided to have a little fun instead. With glowing details, a heroic stance, and the quiet confidence of something that has absolutely seen some things, Mechan 42 doesn’t just stand there—it arrives. It looms. It radiates “don’t worry, I’ve got this” energy, even though no one is quite sure what this is. Friendly, formidable, and mildly mysterious, Mechan 42 is here to protect, inspire, and maybe abduct your imagination for a quick interstellar joyride.Funded in part by the office of Councilor Jamie DunphyJoin us Feb 6 for the big launch party!

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Threshold frames light as a passage, inviting viewers to linger at the edge between arrival and immersion.

Explore the Electric Blocks, a dazzling display of light art, interactive installations, the Silent Disco and food carts. It is a hub of creative brilliance, featuring stunning light-based installatio

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Inspired by unicellular motion, this videomapping magnifies invisible cellular flows into a luminous landscape, revealing life’s continuity.

metadata.description: Inspired by the continuous, fluid movements of unicellular organisms, this installation explores life at its most elemental scale. Observed under the microscope, cells shift, expand, divide, and flow in an endless choreography that mirrors larger natural systems, from rivers to cosmic motion. Through video mapping, these invisible micro-movements are magnified and projected onto architecture, transforming it into a luminous, living landscape. What appears minute and fragile becomes monumental in light, revealing how principles of connection and transformation are embedded in even the simplest forms of life, inviting viewers into a dreamlike visual organism.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Meet the Gate Creepers: playful guardians standing tall to guide you into the Electric Blocks anchor site.

metadata.description: These colorful and friendly stick bugs were originally designed by artist Nicolás Barrome Forgues for the 2024 Pickathon Festival. They now make their PDXWLF debut re-purposed to welcome you to the world of All the Little Things. A world where smaller creatures have grown so big, and now it’s your time to feel small!

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Come in and stare awhile! Sit, relax, and interact with yourself inside of a triangle!

metadata.description: You will always be watched, and through this project, I learned a few things about what that really means. It forces you to sit down and look at yourself—who you are, how you act, and what you choose to share with the world.

place_info.place_name: Join community at The T-Horse at Electric Blocks
place_info.place_editorial_summary: The T-Horse is a mobile tea house that extends its wings to create a temporary gathering space to slow down, sip free tea and create art in community.

metadata.description: People have been gathering around tea for centuries. At City Repair, our mission is to empower communities to reclaim public space. The T-Horse enables us to make a community plaza anywhere we go. The T-Horse is an ephemeral structure to support the placemaking movement, an approach to the community driven design of public spaces that promotes connection, happiness, and well being. This imaginative community project has been lighting up the streets of Portland for over a decade.We serve tea from a structure connected to small truck bed, with wings made of bamboo and reclaimed sailcloth extending in all directions to create shelter and a magical atmosphere to gather around, where we host open discussions and participatory art.

place_info.place_name: The Art Piece Formally Known As A Portal
place_info.place_editorial_summary: Standing on the street you notice an old abandoned wardrobe. Typical for this part of town… but wait, what is that strange glow coming from within?

metadata.description: Originally thought to be a portal to another dimension the art piece formally known as a portal is obviously not what you first thought. It’s alluring waves of ambient color draw you in. Closer. Then closer… soothed by its mesmerizing pulse you are slowly lulled into a false sense of calm. Is it a portal? Is it a human trap set by a visiting intelligence? Is it a collection of light emitting diodes and foam core poster board loving assembled into a form of transcendental meaning? Was it ever even art?

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Infinitesimally small blocks of time, when strung together, create the illusion of continuity and motion.

artists: Fragments of Now at Electric Blocks
metadata.description: Participants will see their own gestures, poses, and dance moves suspended, remixed and glitched in space-time.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Explore the prismatic folds of the Kaleidoscopizer! An interactive kaleidoscope with buttons and knobs

metadata.description: A deconstructed digital take on the timeless kaleidoscope.A multi-color, color-shifting icosidodecahedral orb made from recycled 3D printing waste, captured live through a camera and kaleidoscopized infinitely.Probe the inner workings with built-in knobs and buttons—change colors, alter speeds, manipulate zoom and symmetry effects. Anything the camera can point at can be kaleidoscopized!

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Ancient symbol, modern rhythms. This 7ft LED triskelion pulses and swirls in sync with music.

metadata.description: The triskelion is one of humanity’s oldest symbols, triple-spiral motif dating back to the Neolithic era. This geometry symbolizes the rhythm of nature, continuous motion, and the unity of past, present, and future.Composed of approximately 1,000 LEDs, the structure’s three branches weave together to form a continuous loop where beginning and end merge. Here, that symbolic motion becomes literal through sound. The sculpture reacts to the music, translating rhythm into radiance.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Enjoy discovering all this interactive glowing rainbow cloud has to offer. So much to explore from every angle! So full of surprises!

metadata.description: This rainbow cloud cluster has so much to explore! Is it a whimsical landscape with fantastical giant creatures? A nest of rainbow cloud producing worms? Hi! I’m Nikki- I have been a creative weirdo forever but never considered myself an “artist.” It has always felt unattainable, too expensive and out of my skill level. I focus on creating art that feels healing to the inner child. When I get in my head- full of doubt and stress and disbelief- I remind myself to play like I’m a toddler scribbling outside the lines of a coloring book and it brings me back! It’s healing to me and I hope it brings you joy, silliness and some whimsy! This piece is made of mostly TRASH (plastics, styrofoam and repurposed vinyl).

place_info.place_editorial_summary: The Portland Adult Soapbox Derby is an annual gathering on Mt. Tabor to race home-made art and science cars.

metadata.description: The Portland Adult Soapbox Derby is an annual gravity race of homemade Art and Science cars down Mt. Tabor in Portland. The Derby celebrates it’s 27th Annual Race in 2026. The PASD is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization run by an all-volunteer board and race-day is run by a team of over 150 volunteers. Come join us on Saturday, August 15th, 2026 and watch these homemade creations of Art and Engineering careen down Mt. Tabor for fun, silliness and bragging rights. Attendance is FREE!

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Pacific Northwest inspired moments as viewed through a childs eyes
place_info.place_address: 120 SE Clay St., Ste 102 Portland Oregon 97214

metadata.description: Glimpse invites viewers to pause and lean into the quiet power of light. Each window along the studio front becomes a glowing shadow box—a framed portal isolating fragile, fleeting moments of joy:a shimmer, a flicker, a reflection, a memory.Light becomes the narrator, transforming small details into something extraordinary.Together, the shadowboxes form a constellation of moments in time. They encourage viewers to slow down, look closely, and see how fragments of light connect past to present, memory to possibility.Glimpse is an offering: a celebration of little things—a reminder that what often goes unseen can, when illuminated, hold profound beauty and meaning.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Look closer, do you know place? How might you?

metadata.description: Explore with us the empire of Kublai Khan through the fantastical tales of Marco Polo. Invisible Cities by Italo Calvino invites readers to see cities in a new way, a magical way, through life, death, time, and the imaginary. This exhibit hopes to share this work as a practice of exploring place and being in a new way. Be transported and see deeply, how does this city come to life? Have you been here?

place_info.place_editorial_summary: I think art is the conduit between living things.

metadata.description: This project began with a simple idea:Get people to stop looking down — and start looking up.We spend so much time buried in our phones, trapped by tech that was meant to connect us but often isolates us instead. In doing so, we miss everything around us: the architecture, the flowers, the colors, the people, the possibility of connection.I wanted to interrupt that downward gaze — to give people a reason to pause, look up, and see the beauty that’s already here.Take a breath. Look around. Things aren’t as bad as they seem.It’s all perspective.

place_info.place_name: Cosmic Ocean and the Light Market at Bridge Space PDX
place_info.place_editorial_summary: Featuring the deep sea planetarium, as cosmic creatures swim in starry depths above, vendors, performers, and installations will fill the hall bellow

metadata.description: First Friday PDX is excited to partner with the Portland Winter Light Festival and Gaia’s Gallery this February for a First Friday unlike any other. Inspired by the swirling clouds of the stars, and the strange and mysterious life of the deep sea, A Cosmic Ocean will bridge the abyssal and celestial realms. Featuring the deep sea planetarium, cosmic creatures will swim in starry depths above, while vendors, musicians, and installations fill the halls below.Wander the realm, sip tea, share your dreams with someone new, connect, collaborate, and support local artists.Keep up to date here:FB Link

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Step into a playground of color and light at WONDERLIGHTS, a 5 day immersive light showcase at Wonderlove featuring live music and art installations.

metadata.description: Located in the heart of the Central Eastside is Wonderlove – a food and entertainment venue featuring stunning views of the Downtown Skyline, 9 unique Food Carts, Live Music, Connex Bar, Indoor Lounge, Rooftop Deck, Outdoor Covered & Heated Tent, and Games for the whole family!What To Expect:-3 Installation Art pieces by Beatrox (WonderHeart), Daniel Young (Fire Kaleidoscope), and Moth Industries (Gigahex), available to view for the entire PDXWLF!-5+ Projection Artists showcasing their Visual Art throughout the property on Fridays and Saturdays-Live music by Amryta DJ Collective 5 PM-10 PM on Fridays and Saturdays-Light Vendor Market (2.06-2.07) from 5 PM-10 PM-Family fun for all ages!!Check out our website for more information!https://wonderlovepdx.com/portland-winter-lights-festival/

place_info.place_editorial_summary: An interactive installation that transforms audience reflection into living digital art.

metadata.description: Using cameras, depth sensors, and real-time generative systems built in TouchDesigner and custom ISF shaders, the piece captures movement, sound, and presence, translating them into dynamic patterns of color, light, and motion. The mirror surface acts as a responsive canvas, continuously shaped by audience interaction. Gestures ripple through the projected environment, breaking apart and reforming into waves of digital texture and feedback.The work invites people to step into a mirrored projection that does not reflect their image but reinterprets their energy, revealing how technology perceives, distorts, and remembers human input.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Cumulus Animalis is a cloud of dreams. Hundreds of stuffed animals are suspended and lit from within to capture a universal sense of childlike wonder.
place_info.place_address: 109 SE Salmon St Suite B3 Portland Oregon 97214

metadata.description: Cumulus Animalis resembles the feeling of gazing up at the sky to find clouds resembling all sorts of zoic shapes. The imagination is a powerful tool that far too many of us lose sight of as we claw our way into adulthood. What if our childlike senses of wonder were rewarded throughout our lives? What would we accomplish if we embraced play and make believe? This piece is created with the hope that we can take a break from the stuffy and banal mentality of routine to find time for the kaleidoscopic chaos of our younger minds.If you feel like adding to the piece, we are accepting donations of used (please nothing too dirty) stuffed animals that will be added to the permanent piece!

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A beautiful collection of larger than life glowing leaves hangs above a set of magical Crystals composed of infinite light reflections.

metadata.description: Our piece “Lamina” as it is a collection of plant leaves made from light. In plants, “lamina” is another word for the thin surface of the leaf blade. While the typical plant’s lamina absorb light for photosynthesis, ours do the opposite, instead emitting light from the glowing veins of the leaf. We view these types of deciduous leaves as a beautiful symbolic concept because they represent necessary change.The Lamina leaves hang from above, as if from tree branches, and the Infinity Crystals sit below as if growing from the ground. One exciting part about the Infinity Crystals is that some of the exotic optical forms visible inside a kaleidoscope (from only a specific angle) can be seen in this piece from any angle.ON VIEW & FOR SALE You can find select works by this artist for sale on consignment at official Portland Winter Light Festival merch booths at select anchor locations during the Festival.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Visit the Architectural Heritage Center galleries after hours! Enjoy illuminated stained glass and a new "Electric" Vision for the City exhibit.

metadata.description: View our “Illuminated Iridescence” stained glass in the font windows of AHC.Check out our new “Vision for the City” exhibits at the Architectural Heritage Center. View historic stained glass, photography, and architectural models, including a huge model of downtown Portland and the 1905 Lewis and Clark Exposition. For the festival, we’re restoring an epic vintage electric sign with over 100 bulbs.Entrance to the exhibits is only during daytime open hours Wed-Sat 11 am-5 pm! There is an entrance fee to see the exhibits. Visit visitahc.org for more info – follow us @visitAHC.

place_info.place_name: LIVE Glassblowing : A Gathering of Artisans
place_info.place_editorial_summary: Rachel will be opening her studio to you and showcasing the orange fire light of molten glass. Live glassblowing demos showcasing teamwork!

metadata.description: A fun filled warm night of performances. A sort of dance with glass. A better understanding of how much teamwork is involved and an opportunity for people to gain insight in the process of glassblowing. Come cozy up with us and see how we all work to create a gathering of hot glass.Entrance is through the garage door on Taylor Street.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Spinning, Amazing, whimsical, Mesmerizing and gigantic Chandelier, crafted for local artist for PWLF in 2023, we add parts and pieces every year.

metadata.description: The Jandelier is a spinning, awe-inspiring, mesmerizing chandelier suspended 20 feet in the air at JajaPDX—a luminous beacon of movement, craft, and intention.Crafted from intricately laser-cut wooden pieces, the Jandelier floats like a living organism, its geometry unfolding as it slowly turns. Each rotation reveals new patterns of shadow and light, transforming the surrounding space into a quiet spectacle of rhythm and wonder. Embedded LED lights pulse gently through the wood, warming the air with a glow that feels both futuristic and deeply organic.Built by three local artists, the Jandelier is more than an object—it’s a shared vision. United by a mission to positively impact people’s lives, the artists infused the piece with care.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A larger than life Crystal Cluster, with infinitely repeated light patterns oscillating within!

metadata.description: The Infinity Crystal Cluster features three enormous crystals arranged in a cluster and mounted to a base. Inspired by the natural crystal forms in nature, the mirror geometry produces a mesmerizing pattern of light and dark. We love to feature natural symbols and objects in our art, but rendered on a larger than life scale. In our experience, interacting with these enormous pieces connects the observer with the sense of childlike wonder they had when they themselves were small. What helps make the Crystals so effective is that many people have already picked up tiny crystal clusters from the ground and held them in their hands and stared at them in wonder. Making a giant one allows us all to enjoy that small personal experience together.ON VIEW & FOR SALE You can find select works by this artist for sale on consignment at official Portland Winter Light Festival merch booths at select anchor locations during the Festival.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: This illuminated head produces visualizations of our environment through an immersive LED matrix.
Glimmers in the Sea is a reflective, textile-based seascape ecosystem created by Reflective Society duo Iris Vondell and Vivien Wise.

metadata.description: As fiber artists we draw parallels between the many stitches that form a garment and the many drops of water that fill the seas. Reflective Society uses retro-reflective materials that consist of many small glass spheres which reflect the light back to the viewer when light is shined on it. We are often reminded that we rely on unseen communal processes throughout textile and garment creation– together we create something bigger than each of us. In this spirit we invited our fiber community to contribute to the installation with crochet, knit, or sewn fish. In the Fiber Rhythm storefront we explore the depths of the dark, deep sea in one window and make brighter, shallower waters in the other. Both contain glimmers of reflection throughout

place_info.place_editorial_summary: A light refracting quilt of compact discs silhouetting an entire house front , that wavers and reacts to stories whispered through tube telephones.

metadata.description: With this piece, by mimicking the classic blank-faced facade of the front of a house with a Compact Disc “quilt” whose myriad surfaces distort and seemingly liquefy all they reflect, we hope to suggest the ways in which a home is constructed of an impossible variety of objects, and people and movements and actions and histories that give animation to the blank face by articulating the collective process of a house continuously becoming a home. The quilt itself moves in enchanting ways, causing new, shifting reflections with each stirring of action surrounding the home. The interactive telephone tubes allow viewers, through the light and material responsiveness, to visualize the narratives they tell of their own homes .

place_info.place_name: l’Orto Nostrano Presents: CARNOVSKY’S ATMOSPHERIC No.10
place_info.place_editorial_summary: Nostrana lights up l’Orto, its Farm+Garden, with Milan-based duo Carnovsky’s glowing RGB installation, plus an outdoor bar pop-up and surprises.

metadata.description: For the PDX Winter Light Festival, Nostrana transforms l’Orto into an immersive world of color and light with a glowing installation by Milan-based duo Carnovsky, creators of the iconic RGB series beloved by design enthusiasts since 2010. Their work reveals hidden imagery through shifting colored light, creating layered, dreamlike scenes.We’ve admired Carnovsky for over a decade—their RGB designs have quietly lived in Enoteca Nostrana for years (IYKYK). Last winter, we deepened that connection with Atmospheric No. 10, first shown at Milan Design Week in 2013. Installed on a shipping container, the piece appears as a serene skyscape by day and transforms into a luminous, otherworldly experience by night. Join us in l’Orto for art, cocktails

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Light Blade is a vertical light structure powered by LEDs and custom software, iteratively transforming still images into flowing color and motion.

metadata.description: Light Blade is a permanent light installation integrated into The Geode, a creative building in southeast Portland. Embedded directly into the architecture, the work treats light as an active material rather than a surface or screen.Powered by thousands of LEDs and custom software, Light Blade iteratively transforms still images into continuous motion, producing shifting patterns of color and form. The piece functions as both artwork and architectural feature, inviting curiosity through movement rather than explanation.Created by artist Martin Eichinger, Light Blade reflects a long-standing interest in perception, technology, and how people encounter art in shared public space.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Reliquary for the recently departed

metadata.description: An installation from:THE MUSEUM OF SACRED SUBJECTS AND OBJECTSBIRTH/DEATH ANCESTOR ALTARS forPhilip Milgrom and Corey PearsonMEET THE LIVING ANCESTOR

place_info.place_editorial_summary: An installation and individual artist pieces reflecting wonderful warm summer night memories with fireflies, bugs, nature, glowing windows, candles.

metadata.description: We all have those lovely memories of warm summer nights. The sounds of nature. Light from a backyard fire, lanterns or candles. Bugs in the air and the sound of crickets. All the small things that add up to a lovely evening in the backyard. Past Lives Makerspace is making an installation to capture that feeling. There will also be individual art pieces from our amazingly talented members. Come walk in our “backyard.”

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Exploring the interplay between our metaphysical and physical world, and the ripple effect it has on our environment.

metadata.description: Step inside to see additional illuminated works by local artist Ayla Leisure.Ask about the drink special- a Light Festival-inspired cocktail!

place_info.place_editorial_summary: For 120 years Portlanders have delighted at the magic of light set against the backdrop of childhood memories at Oaks Amusement Park.
place_info.place_address: 7805 SE Oaks Park Way Portland Oregon 97202

metadata.description: Since its inception, light features and light as art have been an integral part of the joy and wonder that Oaks Park has brought our community. Enjoy a walk down the midway and view the artistry of light from dynamic modern ride lights to the charm of our antique attractions. Admission to the midway is free.*Rides will not be operational.*Parking fees apply.*The Roller Rink Cafe is open at 7 PM Tuesday-Sunday.*Restrooms are available near the rink.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: Brief description: Upcycled bicycle components are transformed into flowers and other curiosities to create an illuminated garden.

metadata.description: Inspired by the former owner’s legacy of sharing the beauty of her flower garden with her community, we planted our “bike seeds” along this bike boulevard, hoping to inspire others to enjoy the small joys of cycling and see the beauty in every day objects.

place_info.place_editorial_summary: The Ida B. Wells High School Makerspace celebrates the 50th anniversary of the first ever mission to Mars – Viking!

artists: Ida B. Wells High School Makerspace
Viking Mars Missions Education and Preservation Project
metadata.description: Viking searched for life on Mars in 1976 and the Ida B. Wells High School Makerspace found it! This elaborate artistic representation of the Viking mission Lander (and a surprising life form) was carefully crafted by students with the support of IBWHS Makerspace staff and the Viking Mars Missions Education and Preservation Project, a 501c3 nonprofit. Using wood craft, metal, and found objects, this craft represents lights and a universal question at full size! This artistic representation of the spacecraft is making the original mission crew proud!
